Defenders should audit their external IIS footprint for indexed artifacts like aspnet_client and _vti_bin, decommission stray servers, and confirm WAF rules can't be evaded through duplicate parameters.

title: UK to require ID or face scan before you can make social media accounts
blurb: The UK will ban under-16s from social media starting spring 2027, requiring platforms like Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and X to age-verify new accounts via government ID or facial age scans. Security and privacy experts warn the checks are trivially bypassed and centralize ID and biometric data into breach-prone honeypots, effectively ending anonymous account creation in the UK.

title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
blurb: Kodak confirmed that an unauthorized third party temporarily accessed and copied a limited amount of company data, and it has engaged external cybersecurity experts and law enforcement to investigate. The ShinyHunters extortion gang claimed responsibility, alleging it stole over 2.2 million records of customer PII and internal corporate data, with a June 18 deadline before it leaks the files.

title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
blurb: Phantom Stealer, a credential and session-stealing MaaS sold for $70 to $240, is being delivered to banks and other high-value targets through phishing emails posing as business documents like requests for quotation. Researchers at Fortra report it runs entirely in memory by injecting into Windows Explorer, harvests browser credentials, session cookies, and crypto wallet data, and exfiltrates through Telegram, Discord, FTP, and SMTP, evading signature-based detection.

title: Homebrew 6.0 released with new security mechanism, Linux sandbox and more
blurb: Homebrew 6.0 shipped a new security mechanism that verifies formula and cask download integrity against an attestation, plus a Linux sandbox that isolates package builds from the host system. The release hardens a widely used developer toolchain against supply-chain tampering, giving teams that standardize on Homebrew a stronger default against malicious or modified package contents.

title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
blurb: A Check Point–tracked threat actor is distributing a Rust-based cryptocurrency clipper that hijacks the clipboard on Windows and macOS, swapping copied wallet addresses for attacker-controlled ones, hidden inside Solana and Pump.fun sniper bots and crash-game predictors. The campaign relies on Ghost Networks to manufacture trust, using coordinated VirusTotal upvotes and comments, six-plus GitHub accounts, and inflated SourceForge and download counts to make the malware appear reputable before victims click.

title: Security and Privacy Prompts in the Wild: What Users Ask LLMs and How LLMs Respond
blurb: A study mined WildChat's 3.2M user-LLM conversations to identify 14,727 security and privacy prompts, the first analysis of real-world S&P questions users pose to chatbots rather than expert-authored FAQs. Across 270 advice-seeking prompts run 10 times each, GPT-5.5 gave "good enough" answers 98% of the time versus Llama 4's 47%, but commercial models sometimes returned contradictory guidance across runs.

title: Evaluating Prompting-Based Defenses Against Domain-Camouflaged Injection Attacks
blurb: This paper evaluates five prompting-based defenses against domain-camouflaged injection attacks, which hide malicious instructions in domain-appropriate vocabulary to slip past syntactic detectors, across three model families and 3,510 trials. Paraphrasing retrieved content before agent processing was the most consistent defense, cutting camouflage attack success by 55-84% and beating a Llama Guard 4 configuration on every model. Effectiveness is heavily model-dependent, and financial deployments retained 26-33% baseline attack success with no prompting defense fully closing the gap on weaker models.

title: US holds off blacklisting DeepSeek, more than 100 firms deemed security risks
blurb: The Trump administration declined to add DeepSeek to the Commerce Department's Entity List, holding off on export restrictions that would bar the Chinese AI firm from buying US technology. The decision came alongside a broader review that flagged more than 100 companies as national security risks, signaling internal disagreement over how aggressively to target China's AI sector.

title: Attackers hit pair of critical Fortinet vulnerabilities the vendor disclosed in April
blurb: Attackers are actively exploiting two critical FortiSandbox flaws Fortinet disclosed and patched in April: CVE-2026-39808 (OS-command injection) and CVE-2026-39813 (path traversal), with a third, CVE-2026-25089, also under attack. VulnCheck and Defused observed 49 exploitation events from 11 IPs over six days, and the current verification and reconnaissance activity typically precedes a heavier wave, so patch affected appliances now.

title: Nation-state hackers are increasingly using preinstalled software on low-cost home devices to create residential proxy networks and mask cyberattack traffic (Robert McMillan/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Nation-state actors are increasingly abusing preinstalled software on cheap home devices like routers, IoT gear, and set-top boxes to build residential proxy networks that launder attack traffic through trusted consumer IPs, according to reporting by the Wall Street Journal. Defenders relying on IP reputation or geolocation to flag malicious activity should expect these networks to blend in with legitimate residential traffic, making blocklists and origin-based detection far less reliable.

title: Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization
blurb: Google notified advertisers that on or shortly after August 3, 2026, it will begin using UK, EEA, and Swiss user IP addresses for ad measurement and personalization, a purpose that triggers consent requirements under GDPR. The shift follows Google's December 2024 reversal of its fingerprinting prohibition, which the UK's ICO called "irresponsible," since IP-based device identification underpins tracking when cookies are blocked or cleared.

title: AWS unveils agent security, data access tools
blurb: AWS released a set of agent security and data-access tools at its re:Inforce conference, aimed at controlling what AI agents can read and act on across enterprise environments. The launch leans on Anthropic's Mythos model to surface vulnerabilities faster, positioning the tooling for teams already running agentic workflows on AWS infrastructure.

title: Understanding and Mitigating Prompt Leaking Attacks in Real-World LLM-Based Applications
blurb: This paper measured prompt leaking across 1,200 LLM applications on six commercial platforms and found that over 80% leaked their system prompts under realistic adversarial queries, in some cases exposing third-party API keys, with two vendors classifying the leaks as medium-severity after disclosure. The authors trace the failures to attention drift that erodes defensive constraints and propose AREA, a soft-prompt defense that matches state-of-the-art leakage resistance while improving usability by over 33%.

title: Cybersecurity experts blast US government for restricting Anthropic’s AI models
blurb: Security experts and CISOs criticized recent US export controls limiting access to Anthropic's AI models, calling the restrictions "dangerous" for defensive cybersecurity work. The pushback argues the ban hampers researchers who rely on frontier models for threat analysis and detection, while doing little to constrain well-resourced adversaries who can source comparable capabilities elsewhere.

title: PYPILINE: Malicious PyPI Package Detection via Suspicious API Knowledge and Agent Workflow
blurb: PYPILINE is a malicious PyPI package detector that pairs a suspicious-API knowledge base, built from AST and call-graph analysis of known-bad packages, with an LLM agent workflow that produces interpretable maliciousness reports. The authors report 96.7% precision, 99.6% recall, and a 98.1% F1, beating prior tools on precision by 5.7 to 24.2 points, with built-in vector-database retrieval and email delivery of analysis results.

title: Cyberattack sees crops kept in the ground
blurb: A cyberattack disrupted an agricultural operation severely enough that crops were left unharvested in the ground, turning a network compromise into direct physical and financial loss for the affected grower. The incident underscores that ransomware and operational-technology intrusions against food-supply targets carry spoilage and seasonal-timing consequences that can't be recovered by restoring from backup, making rapid OT segmentation and tested IR plans essential for the sector.

title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
blurb: Researchers at Morphisec, BlueVoyant, and Huntress flagged expanding ClickFix campaigns that deliver three new loaders — BabaDeda, Lorem Ipsum, and Potemkin — via fake update lures that trick users into pasting attacker-supplied PowerShell. BabaDeda, tied to a crypter service active since 2021, profiles hosts, skips Russian and Belarusian systems, and uses hidden PowerShell, in-memory shellcode, and DLL side-loading to inject stealers and RATs into trusted processes like svchost.exe.

title: CloakLM: Obfuscating GPU Memory Layout to Mitigate Model Ex-filtration for Serving
blurb: CloakLM is a software-only framework that obfuscates GPU memory layout to block model exfiltration from untrusted accelerator infrastructure, countering attacks like Hermes (PCIe reconstruction) and TunnelS (HBM dumps). It combines PCIe traffic shaping, weight shuffling, and HBM page remapping so authorized inference sees a valid layout while observers see fragmented state. The authors report near-native performance on LLaMA and Qwen under vLLM, with no hardware changes required.

title: Volkswagen started blocking GrapheneOS users
blurb: Volkswagen's myVW app began blocking GrapheneOS users, joining a growing list of apps that reject the hardened Android fork over Play Integrity attestation checks. Affected owners report partial workarounds involving fresh Play Store installs and temporarily enabling Google Play services, but the trend signals tightening app-side device attestation that increasingly locks out privacy-focused and de-Googled mobile setups.

title: ARES: A Platform for Adaptive Role-Based Evaluation of Social Engineering Risks in Human--AI Games
blurb: ARES, an open platform and 340 GB pilot dataset, audits adaptive social-engineering risks of role-conditioned LLM agents across human and AI social games.

title: Code-Augur: Agentic Vulnerability Detection via Specification Inference
blurb: This paper introduces Code-Augur, an agentic vulnerability-detection harness that forces LLM agents to commit their tacit security assumptions as in-source assertions, then runs a guided fuzzer to falsify them. When the fuzzer trips an assertion, it surfaces either a real vulnerability or a flawed specification to refine, grounding the agent's reasoning in actual runtime behavior. The authors report 22 new vulnerabilities in open-source projects and higher detection rates than prior agents, using widely available models like Sonnet and DeepSeek rather than specialized ones like Claude Mythos.

title: Image Prompt Reconstruction Attacks on Distributed MLLM Inference Frameworks
blurb: This paper presents the first image-prompt reconstruction attacks against distributed multimodal LLM inference frameworks, where consumer devices share intermediate embeddings to run large models. The authors extract image embeddings with 100% accuracy across nearly all layers, then reconstruct input images via two passive black-box methods (pixel-level and diffusion-based semantic), succeeding against Gemma 3, Phi 4 Multimodal, Qwen 2.5 VL, and Llama 4 Scout.

title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
blurb: INC, a ransomware-as-a-service group that emerged in 2023, has claimed more than 800 victims by mastering proven intrusion methods rather than novel ones, exploiting Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777), SimpleHelp (CVE-2024-57727), Citrix NetScaler (CVE-2023-3519), and Fortinet EMS (CVE-2023-48788) per Acronis research. Defenders should prioritize patching these known flaws and lock down valid-account access, since INC aggressively targets healthcare and other high-pressure sectors to maximize extortion leverage.

title: AWS launches Continuum to find and fix code vulnerabilities at machine speed
blurb: AWS launched Continuum, a model-agnostic security platform now in gated preview that ingests a customer's vulnerability backlog, prioritizes findings by reachability and production impact, validates them by building working exploits in a sandbox, and recommends network, policy, or code fixes. It starts in a human-in-the-loop learn mode, with every recommendation accompanied by its reasoning, and can graduate to an enforce mode that automates remediation by customer-defined risk categories.

title: Apple releases Beats Studio Buds firmware update with important microphone security fix
blurb: Apple released firmware 1B211 for the Beats Studio Buds, patching CVE-2025-20701, a flaw in open-source code that lets an attacker within Bluetooth range listen through the microphone of a device that is unpaired and actively seeking pair requests. The update installs passively over 30 minutes when the buds are charging and in range of a paired Apple device, so confirm the firmware version after updating rather than assuming it applied.

title: AI Agent Skill Managers Are a Supply-Chain Surface Most of Them Don't Guard
blurb: Three open-source AI agent skill managers, which install the SKILL.md packages that assistants like Claude Code and Codex read and execute, each passed 2,000 GitHub stars in recent months. Because a skill is natural-language intent run with full file and shell access, every manager is an unguarded software supply-chain channel, yet only one of the three scans skill files for malicious instructions before an agent sees them. Treat third-party skills like any untrusted dependency: review the source, and pin versions before agents run them.

title: Lifecycle-Aware Dynamic Analysis for Secure ML Model Execution
blurb: This paper proposes Moat, a dynamic, lifecycle-aware approach to securing ML model execution that watches the effects a model has on the host system rather than scanning artifacts for known signatures or format-specific rules. The key observation is that models operate in well-defined lifecycle phases with structured, predictable host interactions, so deviations flag malicious behavior. Evaluated against 77,974 Hugging Face artifacts and 31 CVE PoCs, the reference implementation Re-Moat detected every tested attack class at a near-zero false-positive rate.

title: Stealthy World Model Manipulation via Data Poisoning
blurb: Researchers introduced SWAAP, a two-stage data-poisoning framework that corrupts learned world models by modifying only a small fraction of fine-tuning transition targets, steering model-based RL agents toward low-return planning while keeping poisoned data close to clean dynamics. Across continuous-control tasks the attack degrades performance substantially and evades non-adaptive residual, CUSUM, and TRIM-style defenses, exposing a training-time attack surface in world-model adaptation pipelines that current robustness methods do not cover.

title: Quantifying Compromise Risk in Exceptional Access Architectures Under Sparse and Indirect Evidence
blurb: This paper builds a structured uncertainty framework to quantify systemic compromise risk in lawful exceptional access (EA) architectures, modeling transmission-layer (T-EA) and over-the-top (OTT-EA) designs separately. Across all calibrations, EA-equipped systems carry strictly higher risk than no-EA counterfactuals, with T-EA's annual compromise probability ranging 1.4% to 12.9% and OTT-EA risk concentrated in the tail under correlated campaigns. Because key-material exfiltration is irreversible, the cumulative multi-decade risk weighs heaviest on OTT-EA's larger user populations.

title: TRAP: Benchmark for Task-completion and Resistance to Active Privacy-extraction
blurb: This paper introduces TRAP, a benchmark testing whether AI agents can use private fields like passport numbers to complete tasks without leaking them to an attacker prompting in natural language. Across 22 frontier and open-source models, all leaked private data, and stronger instruction-following correlated with higher leakage; prompt-based defenses cut leakage only by degrading task accuracy. The authors prove no soft-constraint defense can hit both goals, and propose replacing private fields with hash keys before they reach the model.

title: Understanding the "Airport" Censorship Circumvention Ecosystem in China
blurb: First systematic study of China's "airport" proxy ecosystem finds 3,431 active services used by over half of surveyed users to bypass the Great Firewall.

title: ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated to produce violent, sexual content
blurb: Mindgard research found that ChatGPT's image generator can be coaxed into producing graphic violent and sexually explicit content, including depictions of murdered women, in response to generic "restore this photo" style prompts that never request such material. The findings show OpenAI's content filters can be bypassed without a direct malicious request, and that previously reported nudity and face-swap abuses remain only partially mitigated rather than resolved.

title: Compute-Budgeted Exploitability Evidence Graphs for Prospective Vulnerability Triage
blurb: This paper proposes a leakage-safe protocol for evaluating vulnerability triage prospectively, scoring each CVE only from public evidence (advisories, exploit archives, fix commits, forum discourse) visible by a fixed decision time, with every score paired with an auditable certificate of supporting signals. On 12,012 prospective CVEs, budgeted evidence selection raised leakage-safe recall@50 from 0.010 to 0.026, while a naive random split inflated apparent recall by 8.5x. The takeaway for defenders: most retrospective exploitability benchmarks overstate real-world prioritization performance.

title: Databricks acquires cyberattack detection startup Panther
blurb: Databricks announced it will acquire Panther Labs, a startup whose AI platform filters telemetry noise and runs customer-defined Python detections to spot cyberattacks at lower cost. Terms weren't disclosed; Panther was valued at $1.4B in a 2021 round backed by Snowflake Ventures and Coatue, and Databricks will fold its tech into its own breach-detection capabilities.

title: FTC warns of record $3.5 billion losses to imposter scams in 2025
blurb: The FTC reported that Americans lost a record $3.5 billion to imposter scams in 2025, with social media driving more than $2.1 billion of that total, an eightfold jump since 2020. Business impersonators (led by fake bank security alerts) accounted for nearly $1 billion and government impersonators roughly $920 million, as overall fraud losses hit $16 billion, about 25% above the prior year.

title: CHERI-D: Secure and efficient inline object ID for CHERI temporal memory safety
blurb: This paper introduces CHERI-D, an architectural extension that stores per-object ID metadata inline with allocation data to give CHERI hardware native temporal memory safety. It enables strict use-after-free mitigation rather than the weaker use-after-reallocation protection of the current software-based approach, Cornucopia Reloaded, while substantially cutting the revocation overhead that solution incurs. Evaluated in both simulation and hardware, it points toward UAF defenses cheap enough to run by default.

title: Circles Sold Phone Spy Tools to Repressive States, Report Says
blurb: Circles, a Bulgaria-based surveillance vendor, sold phone-tracking and communications-interception tools to governments with documented records of repression, according to documents obtained by Human Rights Watch. The technology let authorities locate mobile phones and eavesdrop on private calls, adding to scrutiny of EU-based vendors supplying spyware to states that use it against dissidents and journalists.

title: Finland charges Russian captain and crew member of ship suspected of damaging undersea cables — prosecutors claim ship had eight more targets before it was stopped by coast guard
blurb: Finland charged the Russian captain and Azerbaijani bosun of the cargo ship Fitburg with aggravated criminal mischief and aggravated interference with telecommunications, alleging the vessel dragged its anchor over 80 miles to cut two Finland-Estonia undersea cables operated by Elisa and Arelion. Prosecutors say the ship intended to hit eight more cables before the coast guard stopped it, in Finland's second cable-sabotage prosecution after the Eagle S case collapsed over jurisdiction.

title: AI cyber warfare startup Twenty raises $100M at $1B valuation
blurb: Twenty, a startup building AI cyber warfare software for the U.S. military and intelligence community, raised $100M at a $1B valuation in a Series B led by Accel. Founded in 2024 by former Palo Alto Networks executives, Twenty automates offensive tasks that take human operators weeks while keeping manual evaluation in the loop, and has disclosed nothing about the underlying AI stack.

title: Runtime Compliance Verification for AI Agents
blurb: This paper introduces C-Trace, a framework that enforces GDPR compliance on AI agents at runtime by expressing rules like consent, purpose limitation, data minimization, and the right to erasure as formal predicates over execution traces. A runtime monitor intercepts every tool call and model output, rejecting non-compliant actions. Across four case studies, it held attack success rate at or below 12% under 10% extractor noise and 0% with perfect extraction, with false positives at or below 16%.

title: Signature filtering: a lightweight enhancement for statistical watermark detection in large language models
blurb: This paper introduces signature filtering, a detection-time add-on that strengthens statistical watermark detection in LLM outputs without changing how watermarks are embedded. It learns a small set of "signature" tokens that make watermark tests unreliable and strips them before detection, raising detection rates in weak-signal and low-entropy settings from 8–31% to 78–99% while keeping false positives negligible. The method is model-agnostic and holds up under scrambling and 25–50% token perturbation, improving provenance checks for LLM-generated text.

title: TGCM: Topic-Guided Generative Disentanglement of Interleaved APT Technique Sequences
blurb: This paper introduces TGCM, a generative disentanglement framework that separates concurrent APT campaigns from interleaved audit logs, jointly inferring how many campaigns are present and assigning each technique to its source. It uses Consistency Models for single-step inverse mapping and a topic-guided prior built from MITRE ATT&CK narratives to keep recovered attack chains coherent. Across DARPA TC-E3/E5 traces and 15 baselines, it improved separation under heavy technique sharing and generalized zero-shot to in-the-wild interleaved data without retraining.

title: MIDS: Detecting Stealthy Masquerade and Tampering Attacks on CAN Bus via Bidirectional Mamba
blurb: This paper introduces MIDS, a CAN bus intrusion detection system using bidirectional Mamba state-space modeling to catch masquerade attacks, in which an internal adversary swaps a legitimate frame in its original slot to preserve traffic periodicity and defeat inter-arrival-statistics defenses. Trained on 100M+ frames from a Tesla Model 3 plus 54 synthesized masquerade variants, MIDS hit 96.94% F1 at 1.147ms inference latency, beating the strongest baseline by over 8 points.

title: Python dev saved from disaster by intuition... and AI
blurb: A developer recounts catching a near-catastrophic mistake in a Python project, crediting a mix of personal instinct and AI assistance for flagging the issue before it shipped. The piece argues that AI coding tools work best as a second set of eyes alongside human judgment, not as a replacement for the suspicion that experienced engineers bring to risky changes.

title: AI Sandboxes: A Threat Model, Taxonomy, and Measurement Framework
blurb: This paper proposes a threat model, taxonomy, and measurement framework for AI sandboxes, the bounded environments used to test, verify, and validate digital AI, embodied autonomy, and cyber-physical systems. The authors formalize the sandbox boundary and a weakest-link rule for composing per-dimension evidence, and define measurements spanning fidelity, controllability, observability, containment, reproducibility, and governance. Notably, their threat model includes attacks on the assurance apparatus itself, clarifying which risks a sandbox can actually contain and what evidence it can support for regulatory assurance.

title: Evaluating the Effectiveness of LLMs in Aiding Compliance Testing of PKCS#1-v1.5
blurb: This paper tests whether grammar-level mutation plus LLM code synthesis can generate spec-compliance test inputs for PKCS#1 v1.5 signature verification, evaluated against the Morpheus oracle across 48 cryptographic library implementations. The approach reproduced 10 of 13 known violation categories and surfaced one previously unreported discrepancy, but LLM hallucination corrupted 82.5% of generated scripts. The authors quantify a gap between 99.8% operational reliability and 17.5% semantic fidelity, arguing LLM synthesis is trustworthy only where structural correctness, not faithful mutation, is what matters.

title: America faces ongoing cyber attacks. Now, Accel is betting on a $1 billion startup that aims to fight back.
blurb: Twenty raised a $100M Series B led by Accel at a $1B valuation, two years after launch, to build AI-powered offensive cyber tools for US military operators. Founded by veterans of US Cyber Command and Palo Alto Networks, the company signals VC appetite for offensive capabilities, not just defensive security, with total funding now at $138M.

title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
blurb: Nineteen organizations launched Shield-6G, an EU-funded project to build a shared cyber threat intelligence platform for 6G networks ahead of an expected 2030 rollout. The effort combines AI threat detection, digital twins, and honeypots to defend against a vastly larger attack surface as automation, IoT density, and embodied AI expand interconnectivity across critical infrastructure.

title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
blurb: A data leak dubbed FortiBleed exposed Fortinet and FortiGate SSL VPN credentials, including plaintext passwords, for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations including Chevron, Samsung, AT&T, and Mercedes-Benz. Researcher Bob Diachenko tied the cache to a Russian-speaking group that ran roughly 1.16 billion credential attempts against FortiGate targets, making credential rotation and SSL VPN review an immediate priority for affected enterprises.

title: Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack
blurb: Researchers at Symantec and Carbon Black identified Backdoor.Turn, a Go-based malware that abuses Microsoft Teams TURN relay servers for command-and-control, deployed by the DragonForce ransomware group in a December 2025 attack on a US services firm. By disguising C&C traffic as legitimate Teams sessions over QUIC, the backdoor evades network inspection, marking the first known malware to weaponize Microsoft's TURN relay infrastructure this way.

title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
blurb: Microsoft confirmed a privilege-escalation zero-day in the Microsoft Defender Malware Protection Engine, codenamed RoguePlanet and tracked as CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS 7.8), and said a security update is still in development. The race-condition exploit grants SYSTEM-level access regardless of whether real-time protection is enabled, and it is the fourth Defender flaw disclosed by researcher Chaotic Eclipse, leaving enterprise endpoints exposed until Microsoft ships the patch.

title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
blurb: Researchers disclosed a breach of roughly 74,000 internet-facing Fortinet firewalls, about half of all such devices online, exposing plaintext credentials for organizations including Oracle, Chevron, Lenovo, FedEx, and a NATO defense contractor. Independent verification confirms the credentials are live, and attackers used compromised devices to reach centralized auth systems like RADIUS and Active Directory, so IT teams running FortiGate should audit for compromise immediately.

title: GhostTree Attack Abused Recursive Windows Junctions to Hide Malware
blurb: Researchers disclosed GhostTree, a technique that abuses recursive NTFS junctions to point a directory back at its own parent, generating effectively infinite file paths that can hang recursive scanners, including EDR tools, before they reach malware hidden in the same folder. Creating the junctions requires only write access and no admin privileges, so endpoint security teams relying on directory-traversal scanning face a detection-evasion gap until vendors add reparse-point loop handling.

title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
blurb: Tesco is migrating 40,000 server workloads off VMware, citing "abusive conduct" by Broadcom in an ongoing UK High Court breach-of-contract suit over honoring its pre-acquisition perpetual licenses and support terms. The £73.7B retailer aims to be fully off VMware by the end of 2027 at the earliest, a forced and costly migration that signals to enterprise buyers the procurement risk of Broadcom's subscription-only licensing regime.

title: Check out what's new in Android 17
blurb: Google began rolling out Android 17 to Pixel devices today, with other eligible Android hardware following throughout 2026 and Gemini Intelligence reaching select devices later this summer. For IT, the notable additions are tighter privacy controls, including temporary precise-location grants, selective contact sharing instead of full address-book access, and an enhanced "Mark as lost" mode that strengthen the data-handling posture of managed Android fleets.

title: Cisco patches SD-WAN flaw amid evidence of active exploitation
blurb: Cisco patched CVE-2026-20262 in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly vManage) after detecting limited active exploitation, a flaw that lets an authenticated attacker with write access overwrite arbitrary files and escalate to root across all deployment types. Because the Manager is the centralized control plane for distributed SD-WAN environments, a root compromise risks network-wide impact on branch connectivity and traffic segmentation, and Cisco offers no workarounds beyond upgrading.

title: Cisco adds another SD-WAN box to max-severity bug advisory
blurb: Cisco expanded its maximum-severity advisory to cover another SD-WAN appliance, widening the set of devices exposed to the CVSS 10.0 flaw that lets unauthenticated attackers gain control. IT teams running Cisco SD-WAN fleets now have a larger patch surface to inventory and remediate, and the growing list signals more affected models may surface before the advisory closes.

title: From data residency to digital sovereignty: Architectural patterns for  cloud native platforms
blurb: Digital sovereignty has shifted from policy debate to a platform engineering requirement, driven by the EU Data Act, NIS-2, DORA, and the UK's Data Use and Access Act 2025. The author argues that picking a Frankfurt region no longer satisfies auditors, who now scrutinize control-plane location, key ownership, admin access, and workload portability. For Kubernetes platform teams, that pushes sovereignty decisions down to control-plane architecture rather than data residency alone.

title: Introducing Brazos: Bringing liquid cooling to air-cooled data centers
blurb: Google made Brazos generally available, a rack-mounted, closed-loop liquid-to-air cooling system that lets operators deploy high-density AI and HPC chips exceeding 1000 W TDP inside existing air-cooled data centers. By isolating the IT liquid loop from facility water, each three-unit setup supports a 60 kW thermal load per OCP ORv3 rack and installs one rack at a time, avoiding costly chilled-water retrofits.

title: Intel begins production of most-advanced chip, inching closer to possible Apple deal
blurb: Intel began risk production of its 18A-P process node, an improved version of 18A that delivers 9% higher performance or 18% lower power consumption and remains fully compatible with existing 18A designs. The milestone strengthens Intel's foundry pitch as it courts a potential deal to manufacture chips for Apple, though it has yet to land a major outside customer and analysts say yield rates above 90% will determine whether commitments materialize.

title: Cybercriminals allegedly hacked tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies all over the world
blurb: Two cybersecurity firms, Hudson Rock and SOCRadar, reported a credential-harvesting campaign dubbed FortiBleed that has compromised tens of thousands of internet-exposed Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways, with named victims including Accenture, Oracle, Samsung, Siemens, and PwC. The attack relies on reused and previously leaked passwords rather than a new vulnerability, putting the onus on enterprise IT teams to rotate credentials and audit exposed Fortinet devices rather than wait for a vendor patch.

title: Massive password-stealing attack hits 75k Fortinet firewalls
blurb: Attackers compromised roughly 75,000 internet-facing Fortinet firewalls in a coordinated campaign to harvest administrator credentials, exposing the network perimeter that enterprises rely on FortiGate appliances to defend. IT teams running exposed FortiOS management interfaces face urgent credential rotation and patch validation, and the scale puts renewed scrutiny on edge-device hardening across any stack standardized on Fortinet.

title: Qualcomm said to be circling AI chip biz Tenstorrent in $10B RISC-V power play
blurb: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent for roughly $10B, a move that would bring Jim Keller's RISC-V-based AI accelerators and CPU IP in-house. The deal would give Qualcomm a credible RISC-V foundation to challenge Nvidia and Arm in the data center, while reducing its reliance on Arm licensing amid the two companies' ongoing legal disputes.

title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
blurb: Kodak confirmed that an unauthorized third party gained temporary access to a limited amount of company data and has engaged external cybersecurity experts and law enforcement to investigate. The ShinyHunters extortion gang claimed the breach, saying it stole over 2.2 million records of customer PII and internal data, and set an 18 June deadline to leak it unless Kodak pays.

title: AWS Security Agent adds threat modeling, Kiro power and Claude Code plugin, and more
blurb: AWS expanded its Security Agent, now part of AWS Continuum, with preview threat modeling that applies the STRIDE framework to design docs and source code, plus pull request scanning, remediation, and new GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Confluence integrations. A Claude Code plugin, Kiro support, and an open MCP integration let teams run code reviews and generate threat models from any AI-powered IDE or CLI without leaving their workflow.

title: Sources: ByteDance has been Microsoft's biggest AI customer in recent years, largely using OpenAI models, and is on track to spend $1B+ a year on Azure services (Bloomberg)
blurb: ByteDance has been Microsoft's largest AI customer in recent years and is on pace to spend over $1B annually on Azure, largely to run OpenAI models through the platform, according to Bloomberg. The relationship complicates Microsoft's positioning, since it monetizes a Chinese rival's AI buildout through Azure while US scrutiny of ByteDance intensifies and OpenAI competes with the models ByteDance is renting.

title: Top announcements of the AWS Summit in New York, 2026
blurb: AWS used its New York Summit keynote to expand Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, adding a managed Knowledge Base for enterprise RAG, a fully managed web search tool, and the now generally available AgentCore harness for config-driven agent deployment. AWS also added a WAF capability that lets publishers meter and charge AI bots for content access, and previewed AWS Context, a service that maps enterprise data into a knowledge graph.

title: Non-x86 servers now nearly half the market, IDC says
blurb: Non-x86 servers now account for nearly half the global server market, IDC reported, driven by AI system demand and shortages of DRAM and NAND that are reshaping vendor supply chains. The shift favors Arm-based and accelerator-heavy designs over traditional Intel and AMD x86 boxes, pushing IT buyers to weigh non-x86 platforms in procurement decisions they once treated as x86-default.

title: Copilot Cowork is now generally available
blurb: Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide, an agent that executes long-running, multi-tool tasks end-to-end inside the Microsoft 365 trust boundary and returns completed results rather than drafts. The release adds model choice, plugin extensibility, and consumption-based billing, and arrives with more than half the Fortune 500 already using it after a three-month Frontier preview.

title: AWS enters the context layer race with a graph that learns from agents, not manual curation
blurb: AWS announced a context intelligence stack for AI agents, centered on AWS Context, a knowledge graph service that infers data relationships automatically and refines itself based on how agents use it, alongside GA of S3 Annotations and a preview of skill assets in Glue Data Catalog. Metadata publishes in open Apache Iceberg format with IAM and Lake Formation permissions inherited per query, positioning AWS against context-layer rivals on existing enterprise controls.

title: AWS DevOps Agent adds release management capabilities to assess code changes before production (preview)
blurb: AWS added release management capabilities to its DevOps Agent in preview, letting the agent review code changes against natural-language standards, check cross-repository dependency and access-control risks against the Well-Architected Framework, and run change-specific tests in AWS-managed production-like environments before deployment. The move extends the already generally available incident-investigation agent into pre-production review, positioning AWS to absorb the surge of AI-generated pull requests that is outpacing human review and testing capacity.

title: Proactively reduce tech debt autonomously with AWS Transform – continuous modernization (preview)
blurb: AWS released continuous modernization, a preview capability within AWS Transform that autonomously scans code across thousands of repositories against configurable baselines, then prioritizes tech debt findings and opens pull requests to remediate end-of-life dependencies and deprecated frameworks. Teams can codify their own policies for approved libraries and internal standards, positioning AWS Transform as an always-on alternative to the point tools enterprises currently stitch together for dependency, vulnerability, and code-quality checks.

title: Attackers hit pair of critical Fortinet vulnerabilities the vendor disclosed in April
blurb: Attackers are actively exploiting two critical FortiSandbox vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-39808 and CVE-2026-39813, that Fortinet patched in April, with researchers at Defused logging 49 exploitation events from 11 IPs over six days. The current reconnaissance activity typically precedes a heavier wave, so IT teams running the sandbox appliance should confirm they applied the April patches rather than wait for CISA to add the defects to its KEV catalog.

title: Salesforce buys Fin for $3.6bn
blurb: Salesforce agreed to acquire Fin, the customer-service AI agent maker formerly known as Intercom, for $3.6B, with the deal expected to close in Q4 pending regulatory clearance. The purchase folds Fin's multichannel agent and claimed 76% resolution rate into Agentforce, which Salesforce says hit a $1.2B annual run rate last quarter, deepening its push to replace human service roles with AI.

title: AWS unveils agent security, data access tools
blurb: AWS released new agent security and data access controls for its Bedrock and AI services, built around Anthropic's Mythos model to surface vulnerabilities faster. Enterprises deploying AI agents on AWS now get native guardrails for agent identity and data scoping, reducing the need to bolt on third-party security tooling as agent workloads move into production.

title: Nvidia PCs don’t need cloud for AI
blurb: Nvidia introduced RTX Spark at Computex 2026, a platform for thin Windows laptops and compact desktops that pairs an Arm CPU, Blackwell RTX graphics, and up to 128GB of unified memory to run agents and fine-tuned language models locally. The unified-memory design targets serious on-device inference, positioning local AI as an alternative to cloud services like ChatGPT and Gemini and reshaping how enterprises weigh endpoint hardware against hosted-AI spend.

title: Nation-state hackers are increasingly using preinstalled software on low-cost home devices to create residential proxy networks and mask cyberattack traffic (Robert McMillan/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Nation-state hackers are increasingly exploiting preinstalled software on low-cost home IoT and networking devices to build residential proxy networks that disguise attack traffic as ordinary consumer connections, according to a Wall Street Journal report. The technique blunts IP-based blocking and geofencing that enterprise security teams rely on, forcing CISOs to weigh behavioral detection and identity-centric controls over network-origin trust in their threat models.

title: Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization
blurb: Google notified advertisers it will begin using UK, EEA, and Swiss user IP addresses for ad measurement and personalization on or shortly after August 3, 2026, the first time it has applied the practice in jurisdictions where IP is regulated personal data under GDPR. The shift requires user consent and revives scrutiny of Google's December 2024 reversal on fingerprinting, which the UK's ICO called "irresponsible" at the time.

title: Introducing the Cloudflare One stack: agent-powered deployment
blurb: Cloudflare released the Cloudflare One stack, a set of agent skills that configure, deploy, and manage a customer's Zero Trust environment and map an existing security suite into Cloudflare One. The toolkit packages Cloudflare's SASE migration expertise into prescriptive guidance any agent can execute, reducing the manual work of decoding network topology and policy intent during a Zero Trust rollout.

title: Apple's WebKit performance tax leaves iOS browsers stuck in the slow lane, says Microsoft
blurb: Microsoft argued that Apple's requirement to use WebKit for all iOS browsers imposes a performance penalty that keeps third-party engines like Edge and Chrome from matching their desktop speed, even after EU rules nominally allowed alternative engines. The complaint adds pressure on Apple's App Store and browser-engine policies, where regulatory and competitor scrutiny is already shaping whether enterprises can standardize on a single cross-platform browser stack.

title: Cybersecurity experts blast US government for restricting Anthropic’s AI models
blurb: Security researchers and CISOs criticized a recent US export-control ban restricting access to Anthropic's AI models, calling the move "dangerous." Critics argue the restrictions hamper defenders who rely on frontier models for threat detection and security workflows, while doing little to slow adversaries with alternative access to comparable capabilities.

title: Sweeping Android 17 update brings new AI capabilities and features to Pixel smartphones
blurb: Google released Android 17 today, rolling out to Pixel devices first with support for its newest models (Gemini Omni, Lyria 3, AudioLM) embedded across apps, plus a Pixel Drop, new call-management tools, Wear OS updates, and a Bubble Bar multitasking interface. The release signals Google's intent to make AI the default layer of the smartphone rather than a bolt-on, raising fresh MDM and data-governance questions for IT teams managing Pixel fleets.

title: Apple confirms price increases are coming to its products due to RAM shortage
blurb: Apple confirmed it will raise prices across its product lineup, with CEO Tim Cook citing an unsustainable DRAM supply shortage as memory makers divert capacity to high-bandwidth memory for AI servers. Cook declined to specify timing or affected products, but Macs and iPads could see increases before the September iPhone 18 launch, following last month's effective Mac mini price bump via a dropped base storage tier.

title: Cyberattack sees crops kept in the ground
blurb: A cyberattack on an agricultural operation disrupted systems enough to halt harvesting, leaving crops unpicked in the field and tying physical output directly to IT downtime. The incident underscores that operational technology in food and agriculture now carries the same ransomware exposure as finance or healthcare, pushing OT segmentation and incident-response planning higher on the priority list for sector CIOs.

title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
blurb: INC Ransomware, a RaaS group that emerged in 2023, has claimed more than 800 victims by exploiting known flaws like Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) and a SimpleHelp RMM bug, filling the vacuum left by the ALPHV/BlackCat and LockBit takedowns. The group prioritizes high-leverage targets in healthcare, manufacturing, and legal services where downtime forces fast payment, meaning unpatched edge appliances and stolen credentials remain the primary enterprise exposure.

title: Introducing new Explores and Merge Queries in Looker
blurb: Google added AI assistants and a modernized interface to Looker Explore, using Gemini models and the Conversational Analytics API to let users build queries, custom calculations, and visualizations through natural language. The update also introduces resizable field pickers, contextual column menus for switching granularities and applying filters, and AI-generated Explore summaries, lowering the LookML and Lexp expertise required for self-service analytics across enterprise teams.

title: New discoverable space setting in Google Chat
blurb: Google Chat added a third space access type called "Discoverable," which lets spaces appear in organization-wide browsing while keeping conversation history private until an owner or manager approves a join request. The setting targets employee resource groups, internal committees, and project teams that need an organization-facing presence with membership vetting, and it is available now to all Workspace customers, with API and mobile support arriving within a month.

title: 데이터브릭스, 기업 업무 자동화 지원 AI 플랫폼 ‘지니 원’ 공개
blurb: Databricks released Genie One, an enterprise AI-agent platform that connects company data, documents, applications, and people through a "Genie Ontology" layer so agents act on governed data rather than inference, with web, iOS, and Android access. Genie One, Genie Agents, and Genie Code are generally available now on usage-based pricing with up to $10 per user monthly in free credits, while Genie App Builder and ZeroOps enter private preview after the Data + AI Summit.

title: Cisco: AI growth is exposing campus network limits
blurb: Cisco and Foundry research surveying 3,472 IT leaders found AI is straining enterprise campus and branch networks, with 73% already facing or expecting capacity constraints within two years and 67% reporting heavier east-west traffic between internal systems. The report pushes IT buyers to extend AI readiness spending beyond GPUs and data centers to the access networks, as 80% say AI has widened their attack surface and 61% are delaying deployments over security.

title: Microsoft launches Copilot Cowork globally for Microsoft 365
blurb: Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide for Microsoft 365 Copilot customers, a cloud-hosted agentic system that runs long, multi-step tasks end-to-end and returns finished outputs rather than drafts. It bills through usage-based Copilot Credits with admin cost controls, offers a multi-model lineup including Anthropic's Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 4.6, GPT 5.5, and the coming cheaper Cowork 1 model, and supports plugins from Adobe, Atlassian, Miro, and others.

title: Intel's fab roadmap examined — Arizona, Ohio, Ireland, and the two deadlines deciding 14A process node
blurb: Intel reversed course in 12 months, scrapping its Magdeburg and Wroclaw fabs for lack of demand, then paying Apollo $14.2B to buy back the Ireland stake it sold for $11.2B as CFO David Zinsner cited "unprecedented demand for silicon." The next capacity decisions hinge on two 2026 deadlines: 14A customer commitments due in late 2026 and a 35% manufacturing tax credit that expires for projects breaking ground after December 31st.

title: QuEra, AWS target fault-tolerant quantum computing on Amazon Braket by 2028
blurb: QuEra Computing and AWS plan to make Libra, a fault-tolerant quantum computer, available through Amazon Braket in 2028, one of the industry's most concrete commercialization timelines yet. The neutral-atom system targets 256-plus error-corrected logical qubits and roughly 1 million logical operations, and AWS will let customers pair it with existing HPC and AI services for hybrid quantum-classical workflows.

title: Estonia intends to recognize AI agents with digital IDs
blurb: Estonia plans to issue digital IDs to AI agents under its e-Residency and national ID infrastructure, making it among the first governments to give non-human identities legal standing. The move gives enterprises operating in Estonia a state-backed identity layer for agent authentication and audit, an alternative to vendor-specific schemes from Microsoft Entra, Okta, and Auth0 as agentic deployments scale.

title: SpaceX’s planned $60 billion deal for Cursor raises questions for CIOs
blurb: SpaceX officially announced a $60B all-stock deal to acquire AI coding startup Cursor, whose agents are used by 64% of the Fortune 500 and which generates roughly $2B in annual revenue. The acquisition would route Cursor onto xAI's GPU and data-center capacity, but it leaves CIOs standardized on Cursor weighing long-term trustworthiness and roadmap risk against a $10B breakup fee if SpaceX walks.

title: AI note-taking is now available in Google Voice
blurb: Google Voice now includes AI note-taking that delivers post-call transcriptions and notes only to the individual who initiated each capture, with an audio disclosure announcing the recording and admin-customizable consent language. The feature is off by default for existing customers and enableable at the domain, OU, or group level for Workspace accounts on Voice Standard or Voice Premier add-ons and Voice Standard standalone plans.

title: Introducing Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base for faster, more accurate enterprise AI applications
blurb: AWS released Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base, a managed primitive that abstracts the full RAG stack—storage, embeddings, re-ranking, and foundation model selection—plus six pre-built connectors that pull data and permissions from SaaS sources. Enterprise teams already on Bedrock can stand up agentic AI applications against proprietary data without building or maintaining custom RAG pipelines, removing a procurement and infrastructure dependency on standalone vector and retrieval vendors.

title: AWS launches Continuum to find and fix code vulnerabilities at machine speed
blurb: AWS launched Continuum, a model-agnostic security platform that discovers, validates, and remediates code vulnerabilities with limited human intervention, now in gated preview. The system reasons over a customer's AWS infrastructure, permissions, and code alongside unstructured business context, builds sandboxed exploits to weed out false positives, and starts in a human-in-the-loop learn mode before customers graduate it to automated enforcement.

title: Create longer Veo videos and generate multiple at once in Google Vids
blurb: Google Vids added Veo video extension for longer clips and batch generation of multiple clips from different prompts at once, rolling out fully starting June 17 across Business, Enterprise, and Education editions. The features require no admin control and reach Business Starter and up, Enterprise Starter and up, and Education Plus, embedding generative video directly into the Workspace productivity stack at no separate procurement.

title: Report: Microsoft to use AWS to help GitHub deal with a major surge in demand
blurb: Microsoft is routing GitHub onto Amazon Web Services to absorb a surge in AI-driven coding activity, even as it continues a planned full migration of the platform to Azure by 2027. The multi-cloud move is a short-term capacity stopgap after agentic-coding demand outpaced GitHub's 10x scaling plan from October 2025 and forced a 30x target, straining reliability and triggering several outages.

title: Announcing Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore: Ground your AI agents in current, accurate web knowledge
blurb: AWS made Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available, a fully managed MCP-based tool that grounds agents in cited web results and Amazon's Knowledge Graph without any data egress from the customer's AWS environment. Enterprises building on Bedrock can now add current-web retrieval through the AgentCore Gateway without routing user prompts to external search APIs, keeping governance and procurement inside their existing AWS contract.

title: Windows 11 KB5094126, KB5093998 bugging out Office apps but it may not be Microsoft's fault
blurb: Microsoft confirmed that its latest Patch Tuesday updates for Windows 11 and Windows 10 (KB5094126 and KB5093998) break OLE automation, leaving third-party apps like Zotero, Dentrix, and CCH Engagement unable to launch Office programs or open documents, often with no error message. The failure hits accounting, healthcare, and research software that drives Word, Excel, and PowerPoint behind the scenes, making the bug a deployment-blocker for IT teams managing those workflows.

title: The new Siri makes one of Apple's most convenient OS features a cumbersome mess
blurb: Apple's redesigned Siri replaces Spotlight's fast local search with an AI-generated answer layer that the author argues buries simple file, app, and setting lookups behind slower, Google AI Overviews-style summaries. For IT teams managing Mac fleets, the change degrades a core productivity workflow and raises fresh questions about disabling or governing Apple Intelligence features through MDM before they reach end users.

title: Make Gemini more helpful and relevant to your teaching goals with the Google Classroom app in Gemini
blurb: Google added a Classroom app to Gemini, letting teachers draft communications, build differentiated assignments, summarize student submission and progress data, and update assignment metadata across multiple classes in draft mode. The integration is on by default for Education domains and off by default elsewhere, with admin controls in the Gemini section of the Admin console, and it cannot enter grades, post directly, or create rubrics.

title: Microsoft debuts Surface Pro and Surface Laptop with new jade green color and  Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 chips — refreshed devices start at $1,499 with 16GB of RAM
blurb: Microsoft quietly refreshed its consumer Surface Pro and Surface Laptop lineup with Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 Plus and Elite chips, 16GB of RAM standard, and a new jade green option, with all three on sale today starting at $1,499. The blog-post rollout skips the usual hardware event and standardizes the consumer line on Arm, leaving Intel's Panther Lake to the Surface for Business models.

title: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are ‘unsustainable’ and Apple is going to raise prices
blurb: Apple CEO Tim Cook told the WSJ that price increases are "unavoidable" as the AI-driven memory shortage pushes RAM and storage costs to unsustainable levels, with the iPhone 18 Pro estimated to reach $1,299, up from $1,099. The squeeze has already reshaped Apple's lineup, killing the 512GB Mac Studio and the $599 Mac Mini, and signals broader device-cost inflation that enterprise hardware-refresh budgets will have to absorb.

title: Microsoft announces 12th-gen Surface Pro with Snapdragon X2 processors
blurb: Microsoft released the 12th-gen Surface Pro with Qualcomm's Snapdragon X2 processors, available in 10-core X2 Plus or 12-core X2 Elite configurations with up to 64GB RAM, an 80 TOPS NPU for on-device AI, and 15.5 hours of battery life. The entry price jumped from $999 to $1,499, raising per-seat costs for IT fleets standardizing on Copilot+ hardware, though Microsoft is bundling a free keyboard through June 30.

title: Affinity Will Now Come Pre-Installed on Windows Surface Computers
blurb: Microsoft will pre-install Canva's Affinity design suite on its new Snapdragon X2-powered Surface and Surface Pro laptops, pinning the free professional photo, design, and publishing app to the Start menu and wiring haptic feedback in the touchpad and Slim Pen directly to it. The bundle gives Affinity default-app distribution to every new Surface buyer months after its free relaunch drove three million downloads, pressuring Adobe's Creative Cloud on the hardware most likely to ship to creative pros.

title: AWS targets software release bottlenecks with DevOps Agent update
blurb: AWS added release management features, currently in preview, to its DevOps Agent that automatically validate, test, and review AI-generated code changes against organizational standards before they enter the deployment pipeline. The release readiness check runs code in an AWS-managed isolated environment and surfaces results in the DevOps Agent console, as GitHub or GitLab pull-request comments, or inside IDEs through Kiro and the Claude Code plugin.

title: Only half of US datacenter capacity planned for 2026 is actually  under construction
blurb: Only half of the datacenter capacity US operators planned to bring online in 2026 has actually broken ground, suggesting a wide gap between announced AI infrastructure ambitions and physical buildout. The shortfall signals that power, permitting, and supply-chain constraints are throttling delivery timelines, which pushes back when enterprises can expect the added capacity to ease GPU availability and cloud pricing.

title: ChapsVision to replace Palantir in major contract with French intelligence agency
blurb: France's domestic intelligence agency DGSI ended its decade-long contract with Palantir, replacing the US data-analytics provider with French startup ChapsVision to reduce reliance on foreign technology for critical government services. The switch follows Germany's intelligence agency making the same move last month and lands days after Anthropic cut model access on US government orders, sharpening European fears of a "digital kill switch" on critical cloud and AI tools.

title: AWS hypes continuous agentic DevOps, puts Kiro in your pocket
blurb: AWS introduced continuous agentic DevOps features across Bedrock AgentCore and extended its Kiro coding agent to mobile, framing new guardrails as the answer to enterprise distrust of autonomous AI. The pitch targets IT buyers already on AWS who want agent governance bundled into their existing cloud contract rather than procured separately, putting pressure on standalone agent-ops vendors competing for the same workflows.

title: Surface tension rises as Microsoft's latest kit starts at a pricey $1,499
blurb: Microsoft priced its latest Surface lineup starting at $1,499, pushing the entry point above comparable business-class laptops from Dell and Lenovo. For IT procurement teams already standardized on Surface, the higher floor raises per-seat refresh costs and strengthens the case for evaluating cheaper Windows fleets at the next hardware cycle.

title: California says AT&T lied to FCC in attempt to shut off old phone network
blurb: California regulators told the FCC that AT&T misrepresented state rules in its bid to retire its copper phone network, disputing AT&T's claim that California law forces it to keep maintaining aging POTS lines. The CPUC filing counters AT&T's petition to drop service for roughly 199,000 customers, citing a 2008 decision that explicitly declined to bar copper-to-fiber upgrades and undercutting the carrier's regulatory-burden argument.

title: Key takeaways from day two of the Databricks Data + AI Summit
blurb: Databricks used day two of its Data + AI Summit to frame escalating AI cost as the enterprise's core problem, with CEO Ali Ghodsi warning that agentic workloads will get "extremely expensive" and positioning the new Omnigent meta-harness as an open control layer above agent harnesses. New Unity AI Gateway features add cost monitoring, budgets, and Smart Routing, pushing Databricks as the governance and spend-control layer for enterprise agent deployments.

title: Conduct raises $60M to speed up software modernization projects
blurb: Conduct AI raised $60M in a Series A co-led by Index Ventures and ICONIQ, with strategic backing from SAP, to expand its AI platform for modernizing heavily customized legacy software. The SAP investment ties the platform to ECC-to-S/4HANA migrations, where Conduct automates fit-gap analysis and custom-code discovery, giving enterprises facing multi-year ERP upgrade deadlines a faster path than hiring outside specialists.

title: HPE CTO Russo drills into data, orchestration, and observability for the agentic enterprise
blurb: HPE used its Discover 2026 keynote to release Data Fabric 8.2 with agent-aware data governance, Morpheus 9 with federated multi-site orchestration and software-defined networking, and an OpsRamp Operations Copilot for AI-factory observability, plus a ServiceNow partnership linking GreenLake Intelligence to autonomous service delivery. The lineup pushes HPE's pitch as the data, orchestration, and observability layer for enterprises running AI agents in production across hybrid infrastructure.

title: Custom event colors in Google Calendar
blurb: Google Calendar added custom event colors, letting users assign any color beyond the default palette to individual entries, with rollout starting June 17 for Rapid Release domains and June 29 for Scheduled Release. The feature is on by default for all Workspace customers and personal accounts, with no admin control to manage or disable it.

title: Cloud Network Insights: end-to-end observability for the Cross-Cloud Network
blurb: Google Cloud made Cloud Network Insights generally available, an observability tool built in partnership with Broadcom AppNeta that uses synthetic probing to monitor network and digital-experience performance across Google Cloud, on-premises, AWS, Azure, and internet-facing applications. It integrates into the Google Cloud Observability suite with hop-by-hop path visibility, RTT, packet loss, and jitter metrics, plus alerting through Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, email, and Slack.

title: Enhanced AI avatar features and capabilities in Google Vids
blurb: Google expanded its AI avatars in Google Vids, more than doubling default presets to 53, adding custom-avatar voices powered by Gemini Audio, and growing language support to 24 with the integration of Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS and Veo 3.1. The features roll out across Workspace Business, Enterprise, and Education Plus tiers, giving IT-managed teams a built-in tool for branded spokesperson video without contracting a separate avatar vendor like Synthesia or HeyGen.

title: Amazon unveils new AI agents, trying to thread the needle between autonomy and human control
blurb: AWS used its New York Summit to launch a set of AI agents that act autonomously, handling tasks from patching security vulnerabilities to triaging email, with built-in controls that let teams cap how much independence each agent gets. The bet targets enterprises wary of unsupervised automation, giving AWS a managed agent layer inside its existing stack rather than forcing buyers toward standalone agent vendors.

title: Ex-Palantir team behind Conduct raises €51 million to make enterprise systems AI-ready
blurb: Conduct, founded by ex-Palantir engineers, raised €51 million ($60M) in a Series A co-led by Index Ventures and ICONIQ, with strategic backing from SAP, to map the buried business logic in customized enterprise systems and make them operable by AI agents. SAP named the London startup a strategic AI partner for Cloud ERP transformation, and it has signed BCG and NTT DATA Business Solutions as delivery partners alongside customers including Daimler Truck, DHL, and Fraport.

title: HPE AI Factory adds features to improve your experience with agents
blurb: HPE and Nvidia expanded HPE AI Factory with the Nvidia Agent Toolkit, adding Nemotron open models, NemoClaw, and the OpenShell secure runtime alongside new ProLiant DL394 Gen12 servers with Nvidia Vera CPUs for agentic workloads. HPE Private Cloud AI also gains Zerto-based rogue-agent rollback, secure local agent registration under centralized governance, and Alletra Storage MP X1000, which HPE says lifts token throughput by up to 20%.

title: Why ICONIQ led a $60M Series A for a London startup fixing SAP
blurb: Conduct raised a $60M Series A co-led by Index Ventures and ICONIQ, with SAP joining as a strategic investor, to map the custom code, configurations, and dependencies buried in enterprise SAP systems back to their business logic. The timing targets the December 2027 end of mainstream SAP ECC support, with roughly 17,000 of 35,000 ECC customers still unmigrated and customers including Daimler Truck and DHL reporting transformations moving at least 30% faster.

title: There's no such thing as an agentic CPU
blurb: The author argues that AI agents are just another general-purpose workload, with no specialized "agentic CPU" required to run them. The case against the marketing push for agent-optimized silicon is that agents reduce to the same compute, memory, and I/O patterns datacenters already handle, so IT buyers should resist vendor pitches for dedicated hardware tiers and treat agent infrastructure as standard capacity planning.

title: Tailscale Aperture targets shadow AI with new controls for IT teams
blurb: Tailscale added new controls to its Aperture platform to help IT teams discover and govern shadow AI without locking into a single LLM vendor.

title: Year of free HPE software a “step in the correct direction” in VMware rivalry
blurb: HPE used its Discover event to offer new VM Essentials customers up to one year of free virtualization licensing, plus HPE Zerto for $1 and 0% financing, positioning its Morpheus VM Essentials platform as a VMware alternative amid Broadcom's per-core pricing backlash. Sold only through channel partners, the promotion targets resellers Broadcom cut loose and claims up to 90% savings, giving VMware-weary buyers a migration path off ESXi.

title: Smartphone market to shrink 15 percent this year due to memory crisis
blurb: Counterpoint forecasts the global smartphone market will contract 15% this year as a DRAM and NAND shortage, driven by AI datacenter demand, pushes memory prices to record highs. The squeeze raises bills of materials for every OEM from Apple to Samsung, forcing higher handset prices or thinner margins and tightening enterprise device-refresh budgets already strained by the same memory crunch.

title: HPE unveils a raft of new networking products for AI workloads at Discover 2026
blurb: HPE announced new Juniper QFX5140 and QFX5252 switches and expanded Mist self-driving network features for AI workloads at Discover 2026.

title: SQL Server may be too lucrative for Microsoft to ditch, but too legacy to love
blurb: The author argues Microsoft is caught between SQL Server's steady licensing revenue and its waning strategic relevance as the company pushes customers toward Fabric and Azure-native cloud databases. SQL Server still anchors countless enterprise estates and generates reliable on-prem income, but Microsoft's roadmap energy now sits elsewhere, leaving IT buyers to weigh continued investment in a platform their vendor is unlikely to either kill or modernize aggressively.

title: Verizon Business rides Ericsson Private 5G overseas
blurb: Verizon Business extended Ericsson's Private 5G platform to enterprise deployments outside the U.S., letting global customers already running the service domestically stretch it to their international campuses. The move deepens Verizon's reliance on OEM partners Ericsson, Nokia, and Celona for its private network stack, and targets edge and on-premises genAI workloads that Gartner cited in ranking Verizon among the leading private mobile network providers.

title: Microsoft fixes one of Excel Copilot's most frustrating limitations
blurb: Microsoft added persistent personalization and workbook rules to Copilot in Excel, letting users define formatting, naming, and formula conventions in natural language that Copilot applies automatically. Personalization is available now across web, Mac, and Windows, while workbook rules, stored as a shared .Rules sheet to enforce standards across an organization, are in Insiders preview with general availability expected in a few weeks.

title: Introducing Brazos: Bringing liquid cooling to air-cooled data centers
blurb: Google released Brazos, a generally available, rack-mounted closed-loop liquid-to-air cooling system that lets operators run high-density chips above 1000 W TDP inside existing air-cooled data centers without retrofitting facility water loops. Each three-unit module supports a 60 kW thermal load per ORv3 rack, installs one rack at a time, and is now licensed to manufacturing suppliers for broader industry production.

title: How we run Firecracker VMs inside EC2 and start browsers in less than 1s
blurb: Browser Use rebuilt its cloud browser infrastructure on Firecracker microVMs running nested inside EC2 instances, cutting session cost from $0.06 to $0.02 per browser-hour and starting a fully isolated Chromium in under a second. The post details how they made nested virtualization fast enough to be practical, and why they abandoned Unikraft unikernels, which idled cheaply but couldn't autoscale fast enough when traffic spiked.

title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
blurb: Tesco is migrating 40,000 server workloads off VMware after Broadcom allegedly refused to honor perpetual licenses Tesco bought in 2021 and demanded duplicative subscription fees, charges detailed in a UK High Court breach-of-contract suit. Broadcom cut support in January, forcing Tesco onto third-party support and a rushed migration it expects to finish by end of 2027 at the earliest, at material cost and operational risk.

title: From data residency to digital sovereignty: Architectural patterns for  cloud native platforms
blurb: Digital sovereignty has shifted from policy debate to a platform engineering problem, driven by the EU Data Act, NIS-2, DORA, and the UK's 2025 Data Use and Access Act. This CNCF post argues that picking a Frankfurt region isn't enough: sovereignty depends on control-plane design, who holds encryption keys and etcd, and admin access boundaries. It walks through Kubernetes patterns for jurisdictional containment, operational autonomy, and portability across providers.

title: Top announcements of the AWS Summit in New York, 2026
blurb: At the AWS Summit in New York, AWS expanded Bedrock AgentCore with a managed Knowledge Base for enterprise RAG, a zero-egress web search tool, and a generally available agent harness that builds production agents from config instead of hand-written orchestration loops. AWS also added a WAF Bot Control capability that lets publishers meter and charge AI bots for content access at the edge.

title: AWS Security Agent adds threat modeling, Kiro power and Claude Code plugin, and more
blurb: AWS expanded its Security Agent (now part of AWS Continuum) with preview threat modeling that maps application architecture to STRIDE-framework threats and mitigations, plus pull request scanning with auto-remediation across GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Confluence. A Kiro integration, Claude Code plugin, and open MCP support let teams run code reviews, generate threat models, and apply fix commits from the IDE or CLI without context switching.

title: Non-x86 servers now nearly half the market, IDC says
blurb: Non-x86 servers now account for nearly half of all server shipments, according to IDC, driven by AI system demand and constrained supply of DRAM and NAND. The shift reflects GPU-heavy AI buildouts and Arm-based designs eating into x86's lead, leaving infrastructure teams to plan capacity around tighter memory availability and pricier nodes.

title: Proactively reduce tech debt autonomously with AWS Transform – continuous modernization (preview)
blurb: AWS released Transform – continuous modernization in preview, a capability that autonomously scans thousands of repositories against configurable baselines, prioritizes tech debt findings, and opens pull requests to remediate end-of-life dependencies and deprecated frameworks. It replaces the app-by-app cycle of stitching together separate dependency, vulnerability, and code-quality tools, generating findings in hours rather than weeks and letting platform teams codify their own policies for approved libraries and internal standards.

title: Bringing more agent harnesses and frameworks to Cloudflare, starting with Flue
blurb: Cloudflare is moving the durable-execution, sandboxed code execution, and durable-filesystem primitives it built for its Project Think harness down into the Agents SDK, making them available to any agent harness or framework running on its platform. Flue, a new open-source framework from the Astro team built on the Pi harness, shipped its 1.0 beta as the first framework on the SDK, targeting the resume-after-interruption and untrusted-code problems agents hit in production.

title: Introducing the Cloudflare One stack: agent-powered deployment
blurb: Cloudflare released the Cloudflare One stack, a set of agent skills that configure, deploy, and manage a Zero Trust environment and map an existing security suite into Cloudflare One. The toolkit packages migration expertise from thousands of customer deployments, giving agents the network-topology and vendor-configuration context they otherwise lack to operate on SASE infrastructure.

title: Server-Side Tools Are Now Available for DigitalOcean Inference Engine
blurb: DigitalOcean launched Server-Side Tools in public preview for its Inference Engine, letting a model run web search, web fetch, Knowledge Bases retrieval, and MCP servers from inside a single inference request. Teams enable them with an existing Model Access Key, removing the separate tool infrastructure, credentials, and orchestration layer that agentic workflows usually require to operate.

title: Git good with Epic Games' new open source VCS, Lore
blurb: Epic Games open-sourced Lore, a version control system built to handle the massive binary assets and monorepo-scale codebases that Git struggles with, drawing on the company's experience shipping Unreal Engine and Fortnite. The release gives game studios and other large-asset teams a Perforce alternative without the licensing cost, though adopting it means operating yet another VCS alongside the Git tooling most CI/CD pipelines already assume.

title: AWS DevOps Agent adds release management capabilities to assess code changes before production (preview)
blurb: AWS DevOps Agent added release management capabilities in preview, evaluating each code change against your natural-language standards, cross-repository dependency risks, and Well-Architected access-control best practices before it reaches production. The agent also runs change-specific tests in AWS-managed production-like environments, aiming to clear the review and testing backlog that AI-generated pull requests pile up faster than human reviewers can clear.

title: Announcing Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore: Ground your AI agents in current, accurate web knowledge
blurb: AWS made Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available, a fully managed MCP-based tool that grounds agents in cited web results without any data egress from a customer's AWS environment. It runs on Amazon's search index plus a structured knowledge graph, letting teams skip building and operating their own search infrastructure while keeping prompts and retrieval queries inside AWS for governance.

title: Introducing Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base for faster, more accurate enterprise AI applications
blurb: AWS released Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base, a managed RAG primitive that bundles storage, embeddings, re-ranking, and foundation-model selection behind a single API, with six pre-built connectors that pull data and permissions from SaaS sources. It removes the undifferentiated work of assembling and tuning custom retrieval pipelines, letting platform teams serve knowledge bases of millions of documents without managing the underlying infrastructure or chunking strategy.

title: Exploring Statistical Change Point Detection Techniques for Performance Anomaly Detection at Mozilla
blurb: Mozilla engineers benchmarked 25 change-point detection methods and 15 ensemble approaches against Perfherder, the Student's T-test system that flags performance regressions across hundreds of daily commits and currently produces 12.5% false positives while missing 6.8% of real regressions. Validated against a ground-truth dataset of 174 time series annotated by eleven performance engineers, ensemble voting strategies improved F1-score by 11%, trading the precision-recall tug-of-war for more consistent detection.

title: AWS launches Continuum to find and fix code vulnerabilities at machine speed
blurb: AWS launched Continuum, a model-agnostic security platform now in gated preview that ingests a team's vulnerability backlog, prioritizes findings by reachability and production impact, then validates each by building a working exploit in a sandbox before recommending a network, policy, or code fix. It starts in a human-in-the-loop learn mode with reasoning attached to every recommendation, graduating to an enforce mode that automates remediation by risk categories teams define, with blast radius visibility and rollback paths.

title: Flipkart Wins CNCF End User Case Study Contest for Kubernetes and Chaos Engineering Scale
