blurb: Flipkart won the CNCF End User Case Study Contest for KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India 2026 for its central reliability engineering team's multi-tenant chaos engineering platform, built on Kubernetes and the CNCF incubating project LitmusChaos to run roughly 90% of fault-injection experiments in staging before high-traffic festive sales. The team shipped four custom LitmusChaos extensions, including a DaemonSet-based high-availability model for parallel injection, and contributed five fixes upstream.

title: Cloud Network Insights: end-to-end observability for the Cross-Cloud Network
blurb: Google Cloud made Cloud Network Insights generally available, an AppNeta-powered tool that uses active synthetic probing to monitor network paths across Google Cloud, on-prem, AWS, Azure, and last-mile ISPs even without live traffic. It surfaces hop-by-hop RTT, packet loss, and jitter alongside DNS, HTTP, and page-load metrics inside Cloud Monitoring and Logging, so teams can tell whether degradation is the network or the app.

title: Build and Deploy a Remote MCP Server to GKE in 30 Minutes
blurb: This Google Cloud tutorial walks through deploying a remote MCP server to GKE Autopilot using the Streamable HTTP transport, replacing per-developer local stdio servers with a single centralized endpoint. Because MCP servers are stateless, Autopilot scales them horizontally during traffic spikes, while the Kubernetes Gateway API plus SSL certificates enforce encrypted-only connections. The walkthrough covers the full path from a Python uv project to a secured public endpoint in roughly 30 minutes.

title: AWS targets software release bottlenecks with DevOps Agent update
blurb: AWS added release management features to its DevOps Agent, now in preview, that validate AI-generated code changes against organizational standards, flag release risks, and generate change-specific tests before code reaches the pipeline. The release readiness check runs code in an AWS-managed isolated environment to verify builds and basic user journeys, surfacing findings in the console, on GitHub or GitLab pull requests, or in IDEs via Kiro or the Claude Code plugin.

title: AWS hypes continuous agentic DevOps, puts Kiro in your pocket
blurb: AWS introduced new Bedrock AgentCore features for "continuous agentic DevOps," pitching agents that monitor, triage, and remediate across the software lifecycle, alongside a mobile version of its Kiro coding agent. AWS framed trust as the main blocker to adoption, claiming the new guardrails will catch bad agent actions before they reach production, the same failure class behind several recent AI-deployment incidents.

title: AI referrals to travel sites surge 194% as engagement rises: Adobe
blurb: AI referrals to U.S. travel sites grew 194% year-over-year in May 2026 and are up 2,215% since Adobe began tracking AI traffic in October 2024, with AI-referred visitors spending 70% longer on site, engaging 21% more, and bouncing 41% less. AI traffic still converts 28% below traditional sources, but that gap has narrowed nearly 70% since 2024, signaling higher-intent behavior marketers should start optimizing landing pages and structured content to capture.

title: 80% of ChatGPT product recommendations change when search is enabled: Study
blurb: A study of 20,000 ChatGPT responses by Visibility Labs found that 80.2% of product recommendations changed when search was enabled, leaving only 19.8% overlap with search-disabled picks. Even products recommended in 100% of search-off runs appeared just 15.8% of the time with search on, suggesting brands optimizing for ChatGPT visibility need to win in cited web sources, not just the model's training data.

title: Databricks unveils CustomerLake, its agentic CDP
blurb: Databricks announced CustomerLake, an agentic CDP, at its Data + AI Summit, consolidating customer data, identity resolution, audience building, and activation natively on its lakehouse platform and governed by Unity Catalog. The pitch is a workforce of agents that continuously analyze, decide, and act, which Databricks says can deliver personalized experiences 1 billion times a day, while positioning marketers to sell to customers' AI agents.

title: Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization
blurb: Google notified advertisers that starting on or shortly after August 3, 2026, it will use UK, EEA, and Swiss user IP addresses for ad measurement and personalization, a purpose that triggers GDPR consent requirements and edges into the device-fingerprinting territory the company itself called "wrong" in 2019. Marketers running EU campaigns should expect new consent obligations under IAB Europe's TCF Feature 3 and prepare for the ICO scrutiny that already flagged Google's 2024 fingerprinting reversal as irresponsible.

title: Meta expands live shopping ads and virtual card checkout to drive more purchases
blurb: Meta is expanding Live Video Ads globally on Facebook and bringing them to Instagram, partnering with providers like CommentSold and TalkShopLive, while adding a Mastercard- and Visa-backed virtual card checkout this summer that masks shoppers' real card details. It is also making product feeds foundational to all Sales campaigns, letting its AI auto-assemble the best ad per user to convert in-app discovery into purchases.

title: BlueConic acquires Blueshift as CDPs move from data to action
blurb: BlueConic acquired Blueshift to add cross-channel execution to its customer data platform, extending real-time profiles into action across email, push, SMS, in-app, and web. The deal reflects the shift toward agentic CDPs, where AI agents decide the next best move from first-party behavior and run it across owned channels, and the combined company now serves more than 600 brands including L'Oreal, ASICS, and StitchFix.

title: AI Brand Visibility: You’re Tracking It Wrong
blurb: Most AI brand visibility tracking just ports keyword-rank logic onto a system that doesn't produce ranks, treating prompts as the new keywords and visibility scores as the new rankings. The mismatch matters because LLMs are probabilistic: the same prompt yields a range of valid answers shaped by phrasing, context, and model version, so deterministic tools generate clean-looking data that misrepresents how the system behaves. Prompts like "Best CRM in 2026" also model a buyer with no context, history, or intent, meaning the fix is a different measurement philosophy, not better prompts.

title: Create longer Veo videos and generate multiple at once in Google Vids
blurb: Google Vids added two Veo features: you can now extend existing clips to build longer, continuous videos, and you can fire off multiple generation requests at once to test different prompts and styles in parallel. The update is rolling out from June 17 across Business, Enterprise, Education Plus, and consumer Google AI Pro and Ultra accounts, giving content teams a faster way to produce social and ad video variations in-platform.

title: AI search adoption rises as consumer trust declines: Study
blurb: Consumer trust in AI search fell 28 points in 12 months, from 82% finding it more helpful than traditional search in 2025 to 54% in 2026, even as 70% report using AI search tools more than last year. The share of consumers who say heavy AI use would reduce their trust in a brand nearly doubled to 39%, making AI content volume a visibility liability rather than a neutral GEO play.

title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
blurb: Threads launched "Your Algo" on June 16, letting users privately tell the feed to show more or less of specific topics for one, three, or seven days, while Instagram expanded its "Your Algorithm" controls across feed, explore, and reels. The shift moves recommendation systems from one-size-fits-all to user-tuned, and platforms are betting the added control lifts engagement by surfacing content people actually want to consume.

title: People prefer products with fewer ingredients
blurb: New research found that framing a product as having "just a few" ingredients rather than "many" made shoppers up to 22% more likely to choose it, even when the actual formulation was identical. For CPG and B2C marketers, the takeaway is a copy test, not a reformulation: lead with ingredient simplicity in product descriptions and ad creative rather than defaulting to the now-generic "organic" and "natural" claims that competitors have already saturated.

title: Google Chrome update will close the door on ad blockers
blurb: Chrome 150, expected June 30, will remove the final loophole that kept Manifest V2 ad blockers like uBlock Origin working, completing Google's multi-year transition to the more restrictive Manifest V3 permissions model. With Edge and Opera likely to follow, marketers should expect broader ad visibility across Chromium browsers, which together hold roughly 70% of the desktop market, and cleaner display and video impression delivery.

title: Google Ads to automatically classify conversion-based customer lists
blurb: Google Ads will automatically classify conversion-based Customer Match lists into customer types like existing and new customers starting August 2026, removing advertisers' ability to leave eligible lists unclassified. Misclassified audiences can skew new-customer bidding and retention strategies, so teams should audit their lists in Audience Manager now to confirm Google's labels match their internal acquisition-versus-retention definitions before the change becomes mandatory.

title: 7 AI search shifts you can’t afford to ignore
blurb: ChatGPT and Claude overlap on just 8% of their citations for the same query, with ChatGPT pulling 16% of sources from Reddit, Quora, and forums while Claude leans on listicles (36% of citations) and content that already ranks in Google's top 50 (64% vs ChatGPT's 37%). Brands now have to track AI visibility per engine, since UGC and community seeding move ChatGPT while traditional SEO and listicles drive Claude.

title: Sixty percent of US consumers say 'AI' in brand messaging is a turnoff
blurb: A WordPress VIP consumer survey found 60% of US consumers say "AI" in brand messaging is a turnoff rather than a feature, and 61% can't name a single brand using AI well. After two years and an average 16.6 weekly hours per enterprise team spent chasing AI visibility, the category still has no recognized winner. The takeaway for marketers: build pages that feed AI engines clean data while giving humans a reason to stay, and drop "AI" as a selling point in the copy itself.

title: Pinterest launches AI-powered ad and shopping tools
blurb: Pinterest released a suite of AI tools, including a business assistant that helps marketers build campaigns and an Ask Pinterest app that guides shoppers through product discovery. The business assistant aims to compress campaign setup for advertisers, while the shopping features push Pinterest deeper into the high-intent commerce moments that already make it a stronger conversion channel than most social platforms.

title: Meta adds expanded analytics and AI restyle options to Edits
blurb: Meta added expanded analytics, AI restyle options, sticker and text opacity controls, and new audience insights to Edits, its standalone video editing app. The comparative data and audience features give short-form creators and brand teams clearer signal on which edits drive watch-through, tightening the loop between production choices and Reels performance.

title: LinkedIn adds more digital skills visibility
blurb: LinkedIn now lets users surface verifiable skills pulled from more than a dozen platforms, including HubSpot, Fiverr, and Beehiiv, directly on their profiles. For marketers, this turns LinkedIn profiles into a richer credentialing signal, giving demand-gen and social teams sharper targeting data for skills-based audience segmentation and partner sourcing.

title: What replaces the ultimate guide in AI search
blurb: AI search engines like Gemini allocate roughly 380 words per page for query grounding regardless of article length, which is collapsing the case for the 4,000-word "ultimate guide." Pages under 5,000 characters hit a 66% AI extraction rate, while pages over 20,000 characters drop to 12%, so length now works against generative visibility. The replacement is extractability: every sentence has to name an entity, state a relationship, or make a citable claim, and teams are restructuring from keyword targeting toward problem-first pages built around the specific situation that produced each search.

title: Social media is the leading source of news, per Reuters
blurb: Reuters' 2026 Digital News Report found that social media now outranks TV and news websites as the leading source of news, with TikTok and Instagram gaining the most influence. For brands, that shifts discovery budget toward creator partnerships and platform-native video, where audiences increasingly form their first impressions before any owned channel enters the picture.

title: The hidden UX of payments
blurb: A designer with 13 years at PayPal and Highnote argues that trust in payment products is decided not by branding or onboarding, but by micro-moments teams treat as engineering details: loading states, confirmation screens, the half-second after hitting "send." The piece reframes Nielsen's old "visibility of system status" and "error prevention" heuristics as the emotional architecture of money movement, and walks through cases like instant payouts where stripping all friction actually eroded user confidence.

title: OpenAI launches scheduled tasks in ChatGPT, details here
blurb: OpenAI began rolling out scheduled tasks in ChatGPT, letting users set the assistant to run prompts at recurring times and deliver results automatically. The feature pushes ChatGPT toward proactive, agent-style workflows, giving PMs a built-in option for routine reports and monitoring that previously required a standalone automation tool.

title: Anthropic ships major Claude Design overhaul with design system imports, code round-trips, and a fix for its token-burning problem
blurb: Anthropic shipped a major update to Claude Design that lets teams import design systems from GitHub repos, design files, or uploads, then builds and auto-corrects components against those rules while round-tripping to code. The release also targets the tool's heavy token consumption, repositioning a viral research preview that hit 1M users in a week from a prototyping demo into an enterprise brand-compliance layer.

title: Mastodon looks to newsletters to help revive the open social web
blurb: Mastodon 4.6 added email newsletters, letting writers send posts directly to subscribers' inboxes without requiring those readers to hold a Mastodon account. The feature targets institutional users, journalists, and media organizations, and because Mastodon accounts are portable, it gives creators a way to build audiences they can carry across servers rather than rebuild on each platform.

title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
blurb: Threads launched a "Your Algo" feature on June 16 that lets users privately tell the algorithm to show more or less of specific topics for one, three, or seven days, building on its public "Dear Algo" tool. The move follows Instagram's "Your Algorithm" rollout across feed, explore, and reels, signaling a broader shift from one-size-fits-all recommendation toward user-tunable feeds that both personalize content and lift engagement.

title: TBM 426: The Trouble With Mirrors
blurb: John Cutler argues that the popular product belief in transparency—that teams improve if they can just "see" how they work—misses what people actually want, which is to feel mirrored rather than shown a mirror. His point for PMs is that every mirror has a frame and an agenda, organizational truth gets negotiated and weaponized, and seeing a problem clearly does not mean a team is safe or empowered to act on it. The practical shift is from surfacing truth to helping people feel their perspective is understood.

title: CME Group to sue CFTC over approval of perpetual futures, CEO tells CNBC
blurb: CME Group plans to sue the CFTC over its approval of perpetual futures, with CEO Terry Duffy telling CNBC that the contracts should be classified as swaps under the Dodd-Frank Act. That classification would subject perpetuals to stricter clearing and margin requirements, raising the regulatory bar for the crypto exchanges and DCMs now racing to list them in the US.

title: Illinois Enacts the Strictest Digital-Asset Tax in the US as Industry Group Urges Veto
blurb: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed SB 3019, the Digital Asset Privilege Tax Act, making Illinois the first state to levy a 0.2% tax on every digital-asset exchange, transfer, or custody event rather than on income or gains. The Crypto Council for Innovation is urging a line-item veto of the provision, warning it singles out crypto for treatment with no equivalent on stocks or bonds and could trigger a 50-state patchwork of conflicting tax regimes.

title: ‘One of the most anti-crypto laws in the US’: Illinois Gov. Pritzker signs 0.2% crypto tax
blurb: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed the Digital Asset Tax Act into law, imposing a 0.2% tax on crypto transactions that industry groups have called one of the most anti-crypto measures in the US. Lobbyists and trade representatives are pushing back, arguing the levy penalizes routine on-chain activity and could drive crypto businesses and traders to relocate out of the state.

title: New Rokarolla Android Malware Steals PINs, SMS Codes, and Crypto Wallet Funds
blurb: Zimperium's zLabs documented Rokarolla, a new Android banking trojan that targets 217 banking and crypto apps and ships with 137 remote commands, including clipboard hijacking that silently swaps in attacker wallet addresses to reroute copied crypto payments. The malware spreads via fake TikTok and Chrome sites, disables Google Play Protect through a Play Protect-impersonating dropper, and uses overlay login pages plus SMS interception to capture credentials and one-time codes.

title: Ethereum's Glamsterdam Upgrade Enters Final Devnet Phase With 200M Gas-Limit Target
blurb: Ethereum's Glamsterdam hard fork reached its final devnet stage, locking a 10-EIP bundle headlined by EIP-7732 (enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation) and EIP-7928 (Block-Level Access Lists) ahead of mainnet activation in the second half of 2026. The pairing clears the way for a 200M gas-limit floor, roughly tripling current L1 capacity from the 60M range and targeting up to 10,000 TPS-equivalent throughput under realistic workloads.

title: Fidelity joins Wall Street's race to manage stablecoin reserves
blurb: Fidelity is launching the Fidelity Reserves Digital Fund on Thursday, a money market fund built to hold stablecoin issuer reserves under the GENIUS Act, days after State Street debuted a comparable product. The GENIUS Act forces payment stablecoin issuers to back tokens with cash, short-term Treasuries, and government money funds, opening a reserve pool that asset managers expect to reach trillions as the $320B stablecoin market grows.

title: Kentucky files lawsuits against prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket
blurb: Kentucky Attorney General Russell Coleman filed three lawsuits against Kalshi, Polymarket, and affiliates including Coinbase, accusing the prediction market platforms of running unlicensed sports betting that bypasses state gaming and tax laws. The state cited that 89% of Kalshi's $23B contract volume came from sports wagering, extending a jurisdictional fight with the CFTC, which claims federal preemption over licensed event-contract platforms.

title: Coinbase Teases 1:1-Backed Tokenized U.S. Stocks With On-Chain Dividends
blurb: Coinbase said it will launch tokenized U.S. stocks backed one-for-one by underlying shares, letting non-U.S. customers own, trade, and redeem the tokens on-chain while receiving dividends automatically. The product targets a category led by xStocks, which holds roughly $516M in TVL almost entirely on Solana, and stays unavailable to U.S. users with no launch date, custody partner, or supported equities disclosed.

title: Moody’s rolls out credit ratings on Solana in tokenized asset push
blurb: Moody's expanded its Token Integration Engine to Solana through a partnership with Alphaledger, letting tokenized bond and fixed-income issuers embed the firm's credit ratings directly onchain. The rollout follows Moody's first TIE deployment on the Canton Network earlier this year and targets a tokenization market that BCG and Ripple estimate could reach $18.9T by 2033.

title: Congress Strikes Housing-Bill Deal That Bans Fed CBDC Through 2030
blurb: Congressional negotiators folded a statutory ban on a Federal Reserve retail CBDC into the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act (H.R. 6644), barring any Fed-issued digital dollar through December 31, 2030. The text carves out private, dollar-denominated stablecoins, slotting alongside the GENIUS Act framework and leaving issuers on payment rails to compete for years with no Fed product in the same lane.

title: Coinbase Stakes Out Brokerage Territory With SEC-Registered AI Advisor and Stock Options Push
blurb: Coinbase used its latest "System Update" to push into retail-brokerage territory, launching an SEC-, CFTC-, and NFA-registered AI advisor, zero-commission stock and ETF trading on Advanced, and forthcoming equity and crypto options. The bundle, which the company frames as building "the Everything Exchange," shifts Coinbase's competitive set from Binance and Kraken toward Robinhood, Schwab, and Interactive Brokers.

title: Bhutan sends $34.5 million in bitcoin to Binance, holdings fall below 1,750 BTC: Arkham
blurb: Bhutan transferred 533 BTC worth $34.5 million to Binance, dropping its state-linked holdings below 1,750 BTC to 1,749.96, according to Arkham data. The move continues a pattern of the country liquidating portions of its mining-derived reserves through the exchange rather than holding long-term.

title: Crypto industry aghast at Illinois' new tax on holding or transferring digital assets in state budget
blurb: Illinois enacted a 0.2% tax on digital asset business activity, applying to firms based in the state or serving Illinois residents with at least $100,000 in gross receipts. The provision was added last-minute to the $56B budget signed June 16, takes effect January 1, 2027, and is expected to raise roughly $60M, with the legislature now out of session until a fall veto window.

title: Here is how Coinbase plan to survive the crypto downturn by ditching its reliance on trading fees
blurb: Coinbase used its System Update event to lay out a pivot away from bitcoin-linked trading fees, showcasing derivatives, tokenized stocks, stablecoin payments, lending, and AI products. Analysts flagged derivatives as the central opportunity, noting that options and perpetual futures make up most global crypto volume and could give Coinbase a more durable revenue base than spot trading as volumes stay subdued.

title: FIFA wanted Avalanche's blockchain to help curb World Cup ticket scalping. Here's how it's going
blurb: FIFA Collect launched its World Cup ticketing system on a custom Avalanche Layer-1 chain, built with Modex, to move ticket resale onto FIFA-controlled rails. The model splits entitlements into a tradable Right-to-Buy that grants priority purchase access and a Right-to-Ticket that redeems into an official match ticket, routing secondary-market activity away from third-party platforms like StubHub.

title: Gaming Industry, Tribes and Unions Press Senate to Ban Sports Prediction Markets in Crypto Bill
blurb: A coalition led by the American Gaming Association, tribal groups, and hospitality unions urged the Senate to amend the CLARITY Act to bar prediction markets from offering sports event contracts, directly targeting Kalshi and Polymarket. The June 16 letter argues such contracts evade state and tribal gaming rules, citing an AGA estimate of roughly $1B in lost state tax revenue since early 2025.

title: Kentucky targets prediction markets, puts red state in potential clash with Trump team
blurb: Kentucky's attorney general sued Kalshi and Polymarket for offering unlicensed sports betting, joining a growing roster of states challenging prediction-market platforms over event contracts. The suit pits a state that backed Trump by 64% against his own position that oversight belongs solely to the CFTC, whose chairman has countersued other states asserting exclusive federal authority over event contracts.

title: Kalshi Eyes Broader Asset Classes for Perpetual Futures After $5.5B Crypto Launch
blurb: Kalshi's CFTC-regulated perpetual futures crossed $5.5B in trading volume in their first two weeks, less than a month after the commission approved its bitcoin perpetual contract on May 29. Co-founder Tarek Mansour said the exchange is in talks with regulators to extend perps beyond its 11 crypto contracts into commodities, equity indexes, and FX, a path that would require case-by-case review under Regulation 40.3.

title: Crypto markets wobble after hawkish Fed outlook in Kevin Warsh’s first FOMC meeting
blurb: Major cryptocurrencies fell 1% to 3% after the FOMC held rates and signaled a hawkish path at Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed chair, with bitcoin sliding to $64,150. The reaction underscores how tightly risk assets remain coupled to rate expectations, leaving crypto exposed to further downside if the Fed delays cuts.

title: Trump comments fuel dispute over Polymarket’s $120 million ‘permanent’ Iran peace deal market
blurb: Traders on Polymarket are disputing whether a US-Iran memorandum satisfies the resolution criteria for a $120M market on a "permanent" Iran peace deal, with President Trump's public comments cited as evidence on both sides. The fight highlights the resolution-ambiguity risk in prediction markets, where payout hinges on subjective wording rather than an objective on-chain trigger.

title: Matter Labs Cuts Staff, Pivots Fully to Institutional Privacy Platform Prividium
blurb: Matter Labs, the company behind the zkSync Ethereum layer-2, cut staff and committed the entire organization to Prividium, an institutional on-chain privacy and compliance stack it began building in 2024 for regulated financial institutions. The pivot follows ZK consolidations at Hyli and Botanix, reflecting pressure on general-purpose L2s to find defensible verticals where regulated entities pay for compliance tooling.

title: ‘Biggest spend of the cycle:’ Fairshake crypto PAC’s pick Barry Moore wins Alabama GOP Senate primary
blurb: Fairshake, the crypto industry's super PAC, backed Rep. Barry Moore to a win in Alabama's GOP Senate primary in what it called its biggest spend of the cycle. The result extends the industry's pattern of deploying millions to shape federal races and reinforce a pro-crypto bloc in Congress ahead of the midterms.

title: Bitcoin layer-2s face a bear-market reality check
blurb: Botanix is winding down operations, concluding that programmable Bitcoin "did not work" in the current market and renewing doubts about whether users want BTC beyond a store of value. Bitcoin's onchain DeFi sits below $5B against Ethereum's roughly $39B in TVL, though builders argue demand persists for bitcoin-backed lending, staking, and yield rather than general-purpose layer-2 ecosystems.

title: Fed holds rates steady in first decision under new Chairman Kevin Warsh
blurb: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark rate range at 3.50%-3.75% in Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chair, while updated projections turned hawkish and signaled a possible hike later this year. Bitcoin slipped from around $66,000 alongside US equities as policymakers raised their 2026 rate forecast to 3.8% and lifted core PCE inflation expectations to 3.3%.

title: Stablecoin infrastructure firm Trace Finance raises $32 million Series A, says valuation grew 10x from seed
blurb: Trace Finance raised $32M in a Series A led by CoinFund, with Coinbase Ventures, Haun Ventures, Jump Capital, Paxos, and Chainlink Labs participating, at a valuation it says grew 10x from its seed round. The stablecoin infrastructure firm's backer list spans both issuers and oracle providers, signaling demand for settlement rails as stablecoin payment volume scales.

title: Businesses have started using Kalshi to hedge business risks; Kalshi says institutional trading volume on its platform has grown 800% since November 2025 (Michael J. de la Merced/New York Times)
blurb: Businesses have started using Kalshi's prediction markets to hedge operational risks, and the exchange says institutional trading volume has grown 800% since November 2025. The shift positions event contracts as a hedging instrument alongside traditional derivatives, expanding Kalshi's user base beyond retail speculators into corporate treasury and risk-management desks.

title: BlackRock Launches BITA, a Covered-Call Bitcoin ETF Designed to Generate Monthly Income
blurb: BlackRock listed the iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (BITA) on Nasdaq, overlaying a covered-call strategy on its $51B IBIT spot fund and writing monthly calls on 25% to 35% of net asset value to pass option premiums to holders as income. The 0.65% structure trades upside above the strike for premium, putting BlackRock against YieldMax and Roundhill in the growing bitcoin yield-wrapper field.

title: Fed’s Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Rate-Hike Bets
blurb: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh used his debut press conference to signal the central bank won't tolerate high inflation, prompting traders to dump short-term Treasuries and price in a rate hike as soon as next month. A hawkish Fed pulls liquidity from risk assets, pressuring Bitcoin and the broader crypto market that has rallied on expectations of looser policy.

title: DeFi Lending and DEX Fees Slump as Leverage Drains Out After June Selloff
blurb: Weekly fees across DeFi's largest lending protocols and DEXs fell as much as 65%, with Aave V3 down 60% to $6.72M, Morpho Blue down 60% to $3.27M, and Curve down 65% to $891K, per DefiLlama. Operators attribute the drop to leverage unwinding after early June's selloff rather than a structural break, noting that trailing 30-day fees are still up across most of the same protocols.

title: Ark Invest buys $18 million worth of Coinbase shares, offloads $29 million in Robinhood
blurb: Ark Invest bought roughly $18M of Coinbase stock while selling about $29M of Robinhood shares on Wednesday, rebalancing its exposure between the two trading platforms. The moves came as Coinbase closed down 2.57% at $164.92 and Robinhood gained 8.78%, with Ark buying the dip on one and trimming the rally on the other.

title: Polychain and Coinbase-backed Satori Finance DEX winds down citing ‘unfavorable market conditions’
blurb: Satori Finance, a multi-chain DEX that raised $10M in a 2022 seed round led by Polychain Capital with Coinbase Ventures and Jump Crypto, is winding down, citing prolonged unfavorable market conditions and insufficient revenue. Its TVL had fallen to $1.2M from a 2024 high of $6.7M, and it joins a wave of shutdowns including Bitcoin scaling layer Botanix as capital tightens across the sector.

title: EarnOS launches anti-AI slop app, raises $6 million from 1kx, Circle and Coinbase
blurb: EarnOS raised $6M from 1kx, Circle, and Coinbase to launch Ero, an app that verifies human internet traffic and rewards what it calls authentic digital behavior. The product targets bot-driven ad waste, paying users in tokens for confirmed human activity as brands look to filter AI-generated traffic from their spend.

title: World Chain Bridge TVL Climbs 33% Over Seven Days as Worldcoin Token Posts Matching Rally
blurb: World Chain's canonical bridge TVL rose 32.87% over seven days to about $602M, making it the Optimism Stack rollup's deepest collateral pool, with WLD up over 50% in the same window. Most of the bridged stack sits idle from a yield perspective, with active deployment concentrated in Re7 Labs vaults on Morpho Blue, which hold $32.69M, roughly 35% of the curator's cross-chain book.

title: Court-blocked: ASIC wins appeal against Block Earner over financial product regulation
blurb: Australia's High Court ruled 7-0 for ASIC, finding that Block Earner's defunct Earner yield product was an unlicensed financial product, ending four years of litigation and overturning the Full Federal Court's earlier decision for the crypto platform. The ruling confirms that crypto products promising a return fall under Australia's existing financial services regime, giving ASIC clear precedent to require licensing without new legislation.

title: BitGo stock surges on $50 million share buyback as value languishes 65% below IPO price
blurb: BitGo authorized a $50 million share repurchase program, sending the stock up as much as 20% on Wednesday despite shares trading roughly 65% below their $18 January IPO price. The buyback, covering about 8% of outstanding shares, lands as digital-asset-linked equities slump and BitGo leans on its BaFin-regulated platform to court clients ahead of the EU's MiCA licensing deadline this month.

title: Limitless CEO says no prediction market platform will dominate, citing perpetual futures precedent
blurb: Limitless CEO CJ Hetherington told Bernstein that no single prediction-market platform will dominate, comparing the category to perpetual futures, where liquidity spread across multiple venues rather than consolidating into one. He sized the institutional opportunity at roughly 10x the sports-betting market, framing prediction markets as a fragmented arena where exchanges like Polymarket, Kalshi, and Limitless coexist.

title: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
blurb: Oak Security argues that crypto's mounting losses stem from a mismatch between what code audits check and what attackers actually exploit. Despite the industry tripling its audits since 2022, the largest losses now come from compromised private keys, governance manipulation, insider compromise, and phishing rather than faulty smart contract code. The piece calls for expanding security beyond code review to cover human and operational vectors, the surface where most stolen funds originate.

title: The money flow is the moat
blurb: The author argues that the most durable businesses sit "in the money flow," taking a cut of value moving through a network, and that crypto is the first technology built natively for this pattern. Stablecoins extend it to any startup by letting money settle globally and 24/7 with end-to-end programmability, while network tokens align users, developers, and validators around growing the same shared ledger. The piece frames open rails and public unit economics as the new moat.

title: Illinois Enacts the Strictest Digital-Asset Tax in the US as Industry Group Urges Veto
blurb: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed SB 3019, the Digital Asset Privilege Tax Act, imposing a 0.2% levy on every digital-asset exchange, transfer, and custody event, the first US state tax tied to transactions rather than income or gains. The Crypto Council for Innovation is urging a line-item veto, warning that the law applies even to self-transfers between wallets and could trigger a fifty-state patchwork that undercuts the federal framework Congress is drafting.

title: CME Group to sue CFTC over approval of perpetual futures, CEO tells CNBC
blurb: CME Group plans to sue the CFTC over its approval of perpetual futures, with CEO Terry Duffy telling CNBC that the contracts should be classified as swaps under the Dodd-Frank Act. That distinction matters because swaps carry stricter clearing, margin, and reporting requirements, and a win for CME would force perpetual futures off the lighter futures regime that competitors are using to enter US markets.

title: ‘One of the most anti-crypto laws in the US’: Illinois Gov. Pritzker signs 0.2% crypto tax
blurb: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed the Digital Asset Tax Act into law, imposing a 0.2% tax on crypto transactions that industry lobbyists are calling one of the most anti-crypto measures in the US. The levy adds a state-level cost to digital-asset activity in Illinois, setting up a likely fight over enforcement and compliance as exchanges and payment firms weigh how to pass the charge to users.

title: Fidelity joins Wall Street's race to manage stablecoin reserves
blurb: Fidelity is launching the Fidelity Reserves Digital Fund, a money market fund that manages reserve assets backing stablecoins for issuers and institutional investors under the GENIUS Act, debuting Thursday. The launch arrives days after State Street's competing reserve fund, as traditional asset managers race for a reserve pool that could grow from today's $320B stablecoin market to between $1.9T and $4T by 2030.

title: Kentucky files lawsuits against prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket
blurb: Kentucky Attorney General Russell Coleman filed three lawsuits against Kalshi, Polymarket, and affiliated firms including Coinbase, accusing the prediction-market platforms of running unlicensed sports betting that bypasses state gaming licenses and consumer-protection laws. The suits deepen a jurisdictional fight with the CFTC, which claims exclusive federal authority over these event contracts under the Commodity Exchange Act, with Kentucky noting 89% of Kalshi's $23B contract volume came from sports wagering.

title: Coinbase Stakes Out Brokerage Territory With SEC-Registered AI Advisor and Stock Options Push
blurb: Coinbase used its latest System Update to roll out an SEC-registered AI investment advisor, zero-commission stock and ETF trading on Coinbase Advanced, and forthcoming equity and crypto options, reframing its rivals from Binance and Kraken to Robinhood, Schwab, and Interactive Brokers. The "Everything Exchange" push pulls Coinbase directly into retail-brokerage territory, with tokenized US stocks for non-US customers and a unified global liquidity pool merging its spot, derivatives, and Deribit venues still to come.

title: Coinbase Teases 1:1-Backed Tokenized U.S. Stocks With On-Chain Dividends
blurb: Coinbase said it will launch tokenized U.S. stocks backed one-for-one by real shares, with on-chain dividends paid automatically and direct redemption, available only in eligible jurisdictions outside the United States. The "not a derivative, not an IOU" framing targets market leader xStocks and its $516M in TVL, positioning Coinbase against Robinhood, Gemini, and Kraken in offshore tokenized equities.

title: Moody’s rolls out credit ratings on Solana in tokenized asset push
blurb: Moody's expanded its Token Integration Engine to Solana through a partnership with Alphaledger, letting issuers of tokenized bonds and fixed-income securities embed the firm's credit ratings directly onchain. The move follows TIE's first deployment on the Canton Network and targets a core gap in tokenized assets, giving investors trusted credit data at the point of transaction rather than from a separate terminal or database.

title: Congress Strikes Housing-Bill Deal That Bans Fed CBDC Through 2030
blurb: Congressional negotiators folded a statutory ban on a Federal Reserve retail CBDC into the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, blocking any Fed-issued digital dollar through December 31, 2030. The text carves out private dollar-denominated stablecoins, slotting alongside the GENIUS Act framework and clearing a lane for issuers building on payment rails without competition from a central-bank product for years.

title: Bhutan sends $34.5 million in bitcoin to Binance, holdings fall below 1,750 BTC: Arkham
blurb: Bhutan transferred 533 BTC worth $34.5 million to Binance, dropping its state-linked holdings below 1,750 BTC for the first time, according to Arkham data. The move signals continued liquidation by one of the few sovereign states holding bitcoin on its balance sheet, after years of accumulation through state-run hydropower mining.

title: Crypto industry aghast at Illinois' new tax on holding or transferring digital assets in state budget
blurb: Illinois enacted a 0.2% tax on any business that exchanges, transfers, or stores digital assets for state residents, applying to firms with at least $100,000 in gross receipts and expected to raise around $60M. Added last-minute to the $56B FY2027 budget and effective January 1, 2027, the levy hits crypto exchanges and custodians with a per-transaction reporting burden, and the legislature is out of session until a fall veto window.

title: Parafin lands a Goldman Sachs credit facility to embed lending inside Amazon, DoorDash, and Walmart
blurb: Parafin secured a Goldman Sachs-led credit facility, alongside One William Street Capital Management, to scale embedded small business lending across Amazon, DoorDash, Walmart, Gusto, and TikTok Shop. The infrastructure firm has funded 50,000+ businesses and extended over $35B in offers to date, with most fundings going to repeat borrowers, a sign embedded lending is becoming the default way platform sellers access working capital.

title: Here is how Coinbase plan to survive the crypto downturn by ditching its reliance on trading fees
blurb: Coinbase used its System Update event to showcase a pivot away from bitcoin-linked trading fees toward derivatives, tokenized stocks, stablecoin payments, lending, and AI products. Analysts see derivatives as the durable prize, since options and perpetual futures dominate global crypto volume and could give Coinbase a steadier transaction-revenue base than spot trading as it builds toward an "everything" exchange.

title: FIFA wanted Avalanche's blockchain to help curb World Cup ticket scalping. Here's how it's going
blurb: FIFA Collect is running World Cup ticketing on a custom Avalanche Layer-1, using Modex-powered Right-to-Buy and Right-to-Ticket entitlements that grant priority purchase access and convert into redeemable claims on official tickets. The design pulls resale activity onto FIFA's own chain rather than StubHub or SeatGeek, letting the federation capture secondary-market value and police bots and fraud within infrastructure it controls.

title: Gaming Industry, Tribes and Unions Press Senate to Ban Sports Prediction Markets in Crypto Bill
blurb: A coalition led by the American Gaming Association, joined by tribal groups and the AFL-CIO's hospitality unions, urged the Senate to use the CLARITY Act to bar prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket from offering sports wagers. The June 16 letter argues sports event contracts are an end-run around state and tribal gaming rules, citing roughly $1B in lost state tax revenue since 2025, a figure operators dispute.

title: Kalshi Eyes Broader Asset Classes for Perpetual Futures After $5.5B Crypto Launch
blurb: Kalshi's CFTC-regulated perpetual futures crossed $5.5B in trading volume in their first two weeks, and the exchange is now in talks with regulators about extending the contracts beyond crypto into commodities, equity indexes, and FX. The expansion would run through the CFTC's case-by-case Regulation 40.3 review and reposition Kalshi from a prediction-market venue into a multi-asset derivatives exchange.

title: Businesses have started using Kalshi to hedge business risks; Kalshi says institutional trading volume on its platform has grown 800% since November 2025 (Michael J. de la Merced/New York Times)
blurb: Kalshi said institutional trading volume on its prediction market has grown 800% since November 2025, as businesses begin using event contracts to hedge operational risks like weather, commodity prices, and policy outcomes. The shift positions Kalshi, a CFTC-regulated exchange, as an emerging corporate hedging venue alongside traditional derivatives, raising questions about how regulators will treat event contracts used for genuine risk management rather than speculation.

title: Anthropic Ban Forces Investor Rethink of Political Risk
blurb: A reported government ban targeting Anthropic is forcing AI investors to price in political risk as a distinct threat alongside stretched valuations and heavy capital spending. The episode signals that regulatory and geopolitical exposure, not just fundamentals, can now move AI names, pushing funds to weight policy posture and government dependence when sizing positions in the sector's rally.

title: Kentucky targets prediction markets, puts red state in potential clash with Trump team
blurb: Kentucky's attorney general sued Kalshi and Polymarket, accusing the platforms of running unlicensed sports betting and adding the state to a growing roster of jurisdictions challenging the prediction-market industry. The move puts a 64%-Trump state at odds with the president and CFTC Chairman Mike Selig, who maintain that event contracts fall solely under federal oversight, setting up another state-versus-CFTC court fight.

title: Crypto markets wobble after hawkish Fed outlook in Kevin Warsh’s first FOMC meeting
blurb: Major cryptocurrencies fell 1% to 3% after the FOMC signaled a more hawkish rate path in Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed chair, with bitcoin sliding to $64,150. The reaction underscores how tightly crypto valuations still track Fed policy, leaving stablecoin issuers and trading-focused fintechs exposed to a higher-for-longer rate environment.

title: Bitcoin layer-2s face a bear-market reality check
blurb: Botanix is winding down its Bitcoin layer-2 after concluding that programmable BTC "did not work" in the current market, intensifying doubts about general-purpose Bitcoin DeFi. The numbers underscore the gap: Bitcoin holds under $5B in onchain DeFi versus Ethereum's ~$39B despite a far larger market cap, and builders now expect demand to concentrate in BTC-backed lending, staking, and yield rather than full smart-contract ecosystems.

title: Sources: JPMorgan Chase has stopped its staff in Hong Kong from accessing Anthropic's AI models, following a similar move by rival Goldman Sachs (Financial Times)
blurb: JPMorgan Chase has barred its Hong Kong staff from accessing Anthropic's AI models, sources told the Financial Times, mirroring a recent restriction by Goldman Sachs. The moves point to growing caution among major banks over data governance and third-party model access in a region where cross-border information controls and regulatory scrutiny make external AI tools harder to approve.

title: 3 ex-Palantir engineers raise $60m from Index, Iconiq, SAP for agentic operating system
blurb: Conduct, a London-based startup founded by three ex-Palantir engineers, raised $60M in Series A funding led by Index Ventures and Iconiq, less than nine months after its $11M seed. SAP took a strategic stake and is embedding Conduct's agentic AI operating system, which helps enterprises like DHL and Fraport untangle decades of custom business logic, directly into its products.

title: Fed holds rates steady in first decision under new Chairman Kevin Warsh
blurb: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark rate at 3.50%-3.75% in the first meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh, but updated projections turned hawkish, with policymakers now penciling in a hike to 3.8% by year-end and stickier inflation through 2028. Bitcoin and major U.S. indexes slipped on the shift, as traders weighed whether Warsh, a longtime critic of forward guidance and the dot plot, will rework the Fed's communication strategy.

title: PayPal Ventures folds: how $1.5B in cuts wiped out a 24-year bet on 14 unicorns
blurb: PayPal is shutting down PayPal Ventures, its corporate VC arm founded in 2002, as new CEO Enrique Lores cuts $1.5B in costs and 20% of the global workforce. The closure follows Fidelity International Strategic Ventures winding down in May, and Jefferies is now exploring secondary-market sales of a 61-company portfolio that includes Plaid, Olo, and Tabby.

title: Stablecoin infrastructure firm Trace Finance raises $32 million Series A, says valuation grew 10x from seed
blurb: Trace Finance raised a $32M Series A led by CoinFund, with Coinbase Ventures, Haun Ventures, Jump Capital, Paxos, and Chainlink Labs participating, at a valuation the company says grew 10x from its seed. The investor lineup pulls together a stablecoin-issuer, an oracle provider, and crypto-native funds, signaling consolidation around firms building the settlement and compliance plumbing for stablecoin payments.

title: ECB’s Lagarde says AI could trigger financial crises and calls for Cold War-style non-proliferation governance
blurb: ECB President Christine Lagarde warned that AI could trigger financial crises more damaging than job losses and called for global governance modeled on Cold War nuclear non-proliferation treaties. The ECB has already stress-tested 109 banks against AI-powered cyberattack scenarios, and Lagarde said she will write directly to bank CEOs demanding serious investment in resilience.

title: Fed’s Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Rate-Hike Bets
blurb: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh used his debut press conference to signal the central bank will not tolerate high inflation, prompting traders to dump short-term Treasuries and price in a rate hike as soon as next month. A hawkish turn at the top of the Fed raises funding costs across lending, deposit, and BNPL businesses, squeezing fintechs that built their unit economics around a falling-rate path.

title: EarnOS launches anti-AI slop app, raises $6 million from 1kx, Circle and Coinbase
blurb: EarnOS raised $6M from 1kx, Circle, and Coinbase to launch Ero, an app that verifies human internet traffic and rewards authentic digital behavior. The product targets the growing cost of bot-driven and AI-generated activity, letting brands confirm real users and cut ad spend wasted on non-human traffic.

title: Ark Invest buys $18 million worth of Coinbase shares, offloads $29 million in Robinhood
blurb: Ark Invest bought roughly $18M of Coinbase shares while selling about $29M of Robinhood across its ETFs, rebalancing its crypto-exposure mix toward the exchange. The moves landed on a divergent trading day, with Coinbase down 2.57% to $164.92 and Robinhood up 8.78%, suggesting Ark is buying the dip in Coinbase and trimming Robinhood into strength.

title: PayPal Ventures shutters as company restructuring continues
blurb: PayPal is winding down PayPal Ventures, its corporate venture arm founded in 2016, pausing new investments and exploring secondary sales of holdings through Jefferies. The move under CEO Enrique Lores severs PayPal's front-row view into early fintech startups like Plaid and Anchorage Digital, just as competitors retain venture arms to track emerging payment and AI infrastructure.

title: China AI Lab’s 170% Stock Surge Cements Winner-Loser Pair Trade
blurb: Investors in China's AI sector are increasingly running a pair trade, going long the perceived race leader after a 170% stock surge while shorting the rival seen as falling behind on commercially viable models. The split reflects a market consolidating around a single winner, raising the stakes for labs that miss the window to ship revenue-generating AI.

title: Monument scores £18m seed for cloud banking platform
blurb: Monument Technology raised £18m in seed funding for its cloud Banking-Platform-as-a-Service, exceeding an initial £10m target and combining core banking, payments, and customer channels into a single managed solution that integrates Mambu, Salesforce, and Nice Actimize. The UK provider, founded in 2023, has already opened a Series A at a higher valuation, signaling demand from financial institutions for outsourced, end-to-end banking infrastructure.

title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Seeks $3 Billion Loan for Sydney Project
blurb: Blackstone-backed AirTrunk is in talks with banks for a A$4.3 billion ($3 billion) loan to fund a new Sydney data center, extending its debt-fueled buildout across Asia Pacific. The raise underscores how hyperscale data centers have become a capital-markets asset class of their own, with private-equity owners leaning on syndicated bank debt rather than equity to finance AI-driven capacity demand.

title: BitGo stock surges on $50 million share buyback as value languishes 65% below IPO price
blurb: BitGo authorized a $50 million share buyback, about 8% of outstanding shares, sending the stock up as much as 20% even as it trades 65% below its January IPO price of $18. The custody and settlement provider is leaning on its BaFin-regulated platform to court companies racing to comply with the EU's MiCA licensing deadline at month's end.

title: Court-blocked: ASIC wins appeal against Block Earner over financial product regulation
blurb: Australia's High Court ruled 7-0 that Block Earner's defunct Earner yield product was a financial product, handing ASIC a win after four years of litigation and overturning a Full Federal Court decision in the platform's favor. The ruling confirms that crypto products promising a return fall under Australia's existing financial services regime, requiring an AFSL, and signals ASIC will police yield-bearing digital asset offerings without new legislation.

title: Flagright secures $12.5M Series A to scale AI compliance platform
blurb: Flagright raised a $12.5M Series A led by Infinity Ventures, with participation from Sella Direct Ventures and continued backing from Frontline and Y Combinator, to scale its AI operating system for financial crime compliance. The capital funds an expansion of explainable AI across transaction monitoring, watchlist screening, and case management, plus a deeper US push as institutions replace rigid legacy systems with auditable, AI-native compliance tooling.
